programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The discovery of a dead marine intelligence officer in a motel room sets the team off on a hunt for her killer. Although the town's authorities have allegedly already investigated the crime, Gibbs is determined to conduct his own thorough inquiries - which may lead him to a terrorist cell. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine major is found dead in an underground maintenance room, having scratched out one of his own eyes - and the presence of a webcam suggests someone watched him die. The team investigates with the help of Lt Col Hollis Mann, and links the case to wanted terrorist Mamoun Sharif, an old adversary who is threatening to unleash a chemical attack

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents try to track down an arms dealer who has the launch codes for the Navy's encrypted satellite defence system and is offering them to the highest bidder. In an attempt to win the confidence of the buyer, Ducky poses as an ageing Englishman trying to sell the information. Guest starring Armand Assante, with David McCallum and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A sailor's body is discovered in a disused restaurant, and his death is initially ascribed to an accidental drug overdose. However, Abby discovers a message written in blood on a laminated card found at the scene and realises he was murdered. Meanwhile, Jimmy continues to pursue a relationship with Agent Michelle Lee

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Meanwhile, the rest of the team investigate the death of a marine sergeant who appears to have been caught in the crossfire during a bungled robbery, but Tony and Tim find evidence the victim may have been targeted. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when marine corporal Flegman is found shot to death, and a phone leads them to a hotel room where Gibbs awkwardly stumbles upon his ex-wife Diane Sterling and her FBI agent ex-husband TC Fornell. She had been attempting to call her boyfriend Eddie to break up with him, but somehow Flegman had taken possession of his phone. The team tracks down Eddie's car and discovers a clown nose along with traces of blood and he quickly becomes the prime suspect

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when a mysterious, fast-spreading illness strikes children from military families. While Gibbs tries to determine the cause, Abby and Jimmy partner with the Naval Medical Research Centre in the hope of delivering a cure before the holidays. Mark Harmon stars

The 200th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The widow of a rich merchant stirs up a tempest of lust as the men of Shrewsbury vie for her hand in marriage - and it seems one has no qualms about murdering all his rivals. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Michael Culver and Julian Firth

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The annual St Peter's Fair gets under way in Shrewsbury but a gruesome murder dampens the festivities. Antagonism between local merchants and visiting traders becomes increasingly ugly, and as civil unrest rages in the streets, Beringar is ordered to kill his friend Cadfael, whose investigations are causing King Stephen acute political embarrassment. Medieval whodunit, starring Derek Jacobi, Eoin McCarthy and Michael Culver

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing monk investigates the murder of a pregnant woman and the priest who rejected her confession. At first it looks as though suicide is a major factor in the case, but Cadfael is not convinced and, of course, he is proved right once the clues start falling into place. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Having finally recovered from the death of his beloved Teresa, Sharpe marries Jane Gibbons. Before the honeymoon is over, he is forced to rush off and capture a fort behind French lines - and while he is away a fever outbreak threatens to cut short his new marriage. Sean Bean stars

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A serial arsonist targets sites related to the 1983 general election. After risking his life in a burning building following a quiet word from Keats, Ray gets personally involved in the high-profile investigation - which forces him to face his traumatic past as it becomes clear the tragic events of the Falklands War are driving the firestarter to murder. Fantasy drama, with Dean Andrews and Joe Absolom (Doc Martin)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene jeopardises a covert operation into a drug cartel when he learns several dealers have been murdered recently and the crimes are linked to an old adversary. Chris falls for the attractive undercover officer, but his feelings prompt a violent outburst that threatens his future in the police, and Alex gathers more clues about why she remains in the 1980s. With guest star Zoe Telford (Criminal Justice)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer's visiting American cousin flees the UK with the loot from a raid on Wakering Hall. Feature-length Christmas special from 1993, guest starring John Gielgud and Ken Kercheval

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A groom-to-be is shot dead while on a hunting excursion during his stag trip. Though apparently an accident, none of his accompanying friends will admit to firing the bullet. Neill Rea stars

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: An unidentified woman is killed outside an air force base commanded by Phryne's old flame Lyle Compton, leading to tensions between the military and the Melbourne police

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective uncovers a tale of forbidden romance as he investigates the death of a man from Toronto's Jewish ghetto. Ogden concludes the victim, Nathan Siebold, died of as a result of a consistent and lengthy exposure to arsenic, and though still alive the object of his affections, Devra Bergleman, is also showing symptoms of the toxin. She adored Nathan, though apparently had been held away for him in preference to another suitor. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective investigates the savage murder of a prominent banker and his two large dogs, apparently carried out by a wild beast. However, a link made with other unexplained deaths suggests the killing is not as random as it initially seems. Meanwhile, Ogden tells Murdoch he must either make a commitment to her and her son or end their relationship. Yannick Bisson and Helene Joy star

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The detective and sidekick Jardine investigate when a prominent loan shark dies after being brutally attacked with an axe. Jardine starts a relationship with an actress who is playing Lizzie Borden in the studio that owns the apparent murder weapon as the duo come to believe it may be a revenge killing - and that there could be a link with Glasgow's Garden Festival. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Mark McManus and James Macpherson

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When the body of a woman is found, all evidence points to suicide - but Havers is adamant foul play has occurred. Reinstated to the rank of Detective Sergeant and paired once more with Lynley, she sets out to uncover the mysteries surrounding the victim. The trail leads the pair to an exclusive club where Havers goes undercover to delve beneath the respectable surface and find the killer. Jenny Agutter guest stars, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will is horrified to learn Matt is pressing for a charge of attempted murder against Emma and sets out to expose the control freak's vicious bullying tendencies with help from former victim Nina Lloyd. Dan takes pity on a drug addict who takes him hostage during a raid on a crack house and offers to help her by securing a place on a rehabilitation programme

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted claims to know the location of a valuable emerald necklace hidden on the site. Classic comedy following life at Maplin's holiday camp, with Paul Shane

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar readies itself for Christmas 1960 with a festive bus trip to see the Regent Street lights, while a visit from a BBC film crew promises to make it a festive holiday to remember. However, it's a difficult time for grieving mother Iris, bringing back memories of her long-departed baby daughter. At Nonnatus, things are tense as Sister Monica Joan determines to bring the Christmas spirit early - despite Sister Evangelina's disgust. As the community prepares for a televised carol concert, it becomes apparent that Sister Monica Joan has gone missing. Jenny Agutter and Judy Parfitt star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Easter approaches in the spring of 1961, and Patsy helps to deliver a baby that is born with severe deformities. Her husband struggles to cope with his daughter's needs, but the sisters rally round to support the family. Trixie teaches a keep fit class which results in a medical emergency, leaving her determined to teach her patients to understand their own bodies

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara treats a pregnant woman whose dockworker husband is struggling to support her as opportunities for work dry up. She is forced into a difficult decision that leaves Barbara facing the biggest test of her career. Nurse Crane meets a kindred spirit when she learns to speak Spanish, and Sister Evangelina helps a woman with breastfeeding, leading to unforeseen consequences

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A rare disease spreads around Poplar Street, and Patsy and Dr Turner try to trace the outbreak to its source. Suspicion over the identity of the first carrier threatens to divide a previously close-knit family. Sister Winifred is forced to confront her prejudices while treating a patient, and Barbara worries about how Trixie will react when she agrees to go to dinner with Tom

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is seconded to a maternity ward, and is impressed by how modern technology is being used to help the mothers - but is left with a moral dilemma after assisting on a traumatic birth. A young man from Poplar is delighted to be accepted into university, but his mother is less than happy. His dreams are jeopardised when his girlfriend gets pregnant, a development that puts pressure on the whole family

programme.title: The Da Vinci Code (2006)
programme.desc: A respected academic goes on the run when he is framed for the murder of a French historian. Pursued by the detective on the case and a sinister assassin monk, he strives to unmask the culprit, leading to a conspiracy determined to preserve a 2,000-year-old secret at any cost. Thriller based on Dan Brown's novel, starring Tom Hanks, Audrey Tautou, Ian McKellen, Paul Bettany and Jean Reno

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony starts taking life as a postman much too seriously for Deborah, who pines for the carefree, stupid layabout she fell in love with. Gary fails to face up to his responsibilities by not telling staff the office is closing down, and Dorothy trains for imminent motherhood by caring for the every need of another helpless, flatulent, breast-fixated baby

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part crime drama. Connor and Walker fear for the children's safety as their suspicions about Harwood are confirmed, and when they finally catch up with him it seems he has lost his mind. David Hayman, Victoria Smurfit and Charles Dance star

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah is closer to George than ever after her triumph at the Swansons' party. However, their happiness is short-lived when they are faced with Regina's bombshell, which sends shockwaves through Inverness. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah arrives in Paris, hoping to reconnect with her husband, and is shocked by what she finds. Elizabeth pushes George to be truthful, but causes more damage to their already fragile relationship. Regina puts the next stage of her plan into action, and while Anna finds out the truth about her real father, Olivia proves the depths she is prepared to go to in order to save her marriage

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey have a new sideline in double-glazing, and a new, ruthless saleswoman in Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sam, Diane and Nikki see a young woman fall from a walkway on the Cole Lane estate and learn that the woman's alcoholic husband holds himself responsible for her injuries. Meanwhile, Mickey sets up an underground operation to investigate the activities of a pub card-sharp. Pooja Shah guest stars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna finds her father drunk ahead of his operation, but Ric quickly sees through her efforts to cover for him and cancels the surgery. Connie hopes to impress the hospital chairman, but knows having Jac in theatre will hinder her chances, and Daisha stands up for Oliver when Linden accuses him of being the ward joker, even though he has proved himself capable of doing the job

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tess treats a woman who has been severely beaten, and attempts to find out how she came by her injuries, while Simon and Lara celebrate their stag and hen nights, but end up at the same venue. It soon transpires that the bride-to-be isn't going to let Simon out of her sight. Bex tries to get back together with Luke, but he ends up kissing Claire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A murder hunt is launched when Reshna's body is pulled out of the Thames and evidence emerges that she was having an affair with a fellow student. Meanwhile, Diane and Mickey are left in the dark when an eccentric mugging victim claims to be an undercover officer and Scotland Yard refuse to confirm or deny her story. Jenny Eclair stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Alan surprises Carol with a suggestion about their future, while David mulls over his own past with Carol. Gita is shocked when Guppy turns up at her flat with Meena

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's Carol and Alan's anniversary. Ted plans an opening party for the new shop and Ian asks David to walk Cindy home

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Having finally been convinced of Regina's duplicity, George warns Elizabeth to be careful of giving the game away. James takes Harry to a private party in Sydney, but Henry is furious. Roy continues to act as Sarah's protector and is on hand when Gordon Walsh comes calling

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Shelagh are at the forefront of the advances in medical technology, and begin a campaign to ensure better respiratory health for their patients. However, Timothy fears their own lifestyle is not good for them and tries to encourage his parents to give up smoking. Violet injures her back and is forced to leave the shop in Fred's hands, a decision that strengthens their relationship but which proves to have other, unforeseen consequences. A new mother's past comes to light, with devastating repercussions for her family. Nurse Crane draws on her own experiences to help her

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is rocked by a series of violent attacks on women, and a prostitute is too ashamed to report what she knows - but her actions lead to a dangerous man roaming the streets, with devastating consequences for the residents of Nonnatus House. A mother tries to keep her daughter's pregnancy a secret to avoid gossip, putting her life in danger. The Turners go on a long overdue holiday, but the weather ruins their plans

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The introduction of the contraceptive pill is welcomed by Dr Turner, but some of his colleagues are less keen on the idea. Patsy tries to help a bargee who wants to give birth among her own community rather than in a maternity ward. Nurse Crane deals with an emergency during a storm, and a familiar face returns to Nonnatus House - but has been changed by her time away

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lenny hears a voice from above, and decides he must wait patiently for more information, leaving the gang to make sure he is well-equipped to receive messages. Comedian Bobby Ball returns as the gullible pickle factory worker, with Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A police informant resurfaces after 17 years on the run, and claims to have details implicating a crook-turned-celebrity in an unsolved armed robbery. Sandra accompanies Strickland to the Hero of the Year Awards, where the recipient of the title is an old rival of Gerry's - who is brought in to work on the case. With guest star Steven Berkoff

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Three highly skilled Chinese agents arrive in London, so the team sets out to discover the purpose of their visit. Ruth goes undercover at a major research corporation, where she learns the trio are looking for a scientist who has discovered a new formula worth billions, so Beth enlists a mole to infiltrate the Chinese security services. However, his trustworthiness is soon called into question. Colin Salmon guest stars, with Nicola Walker and Sophia Myles

programme.desc: Feature-length drama set in the 1950s, starring Emilia Fox as the rebellious teenage daughter of a successful businessman and his devoted wife, whose disenchantment with her privileged upbringing ultimately provokes a scandal in their small town. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: Football Focus

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair in tonight's episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Comedian Omid Djalili, reality star Charlotte Crosby, TV presenter Dr Zoe Williams and former England rugby international James Haskell answer questions on the Harry Potter Films, Curb Your Enthusiasm, Nelson Mandela, and Whales, Dolphins and Porpoises

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees Bill, HRVY, Jamie and Maisie perform a dance chosen by the judges, their showdance and their favourite routine from the series. The people with the paddles - Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse - will critique each performance, but it will be up to the viewers alone to determine who will be taking the glitterball from reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher. There'll also be a performance by pop superstar Robbie Williams

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize, guided by tonight's seven celebrities, who include Chris Kamara, Gyles Brandreth and Carol Vorderman

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park, Southampton v Manchester City at St Mary's Stadium, Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park and Newcastle United v Fulham at St James' Park

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present the latest action, including the Thursday night match between Las Vegas Raiders and Los Angeles Chargers

programme.title: Notes on a Scandal (2006)
programme.desc: Lonely history teacher Barbara Covett's acerbic personality means she has few friends. When a spirited woman takes a job in the art department of the school where Barbara works, the elderly lady develops a dark obsession with her new colleague, and the discovery of an affair with a student sets her on a mission to destroy the life of her new 'friend'. Drama, starring Judi Dench, Cate Blanchett, Bill Nighy, Tom Georgeson and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park, Southampton v Manchester City at St Mary's Stadium, Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park and Newcastle United v Fulham at St James' Park

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Cornish Christmas traditions, visiting the cathedral where Nine Lessons and Carols first began - with the intention of getting people out of pubs and into church

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the fourth Sunday of Advent, the Rev Canon Roland Riem leads the morning worship from Winchester Cathedral. With carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a special performance from the boys choir Libera

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: Interviews with the Olympic athlete, in which he discusses his faith, his current work as a coach for Team GB and the failed drugs test that marred the end of his career

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is joined by Cliff Richard, Katherine Jenkins and the Priests at St Paul's Cathedral to reveal the results of a viewers' poll to select the nation's top 10 carols. The winning song will be performed live by opera singer Andrea Bocelli, while a panel of experts including Gareth Malone, Pam Rhodes, Karen Gibson, Mark De-Lisser and Andrew Carwood, explain the fascinating stories and histories behind the songs featured

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Kristin Scott Thomas makes an appeal on behalf of Winston's Wish, a charity providing support for bereaved children and their families

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne calls on help to exercise creative flair to an outdated armchair, while blacksmiths Bex and Dave are tasked with turning old scaffolding props into something special

programme.title: Hammy's Boomerang Adventure
programme.desc: Over the Hedge short spin-off. RJ the raccoon gets to work on producing a nature film, which soon turns out to be an excuse to play a series of pranks on Hammy the squirrel. Featuring the voices of Bruce Willis, Steve Carell and Garry Shandling

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live on the coast, including sea lions working in packs to hunt tuna in the shallows and moray eels that venture out of the water to seek out food on dry rocks. Puffins must brave predators to bring a mouthful of food back to their young and king penguins go without food for a month while they moult. Plus, footage of the bizarre Leaping Blenny, a fish that hates the water

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team celebrates Christmas on the Auchlyne estate in Scotland. Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley join laird Emma and her family to help monitor beavers and tame some deer. Matt Baker makes decorations for a special Christmas tree that will help birds survive the winter cold, and John Craven finds out about the ancient tradition of Gaelic psalm singing

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Minehead Railway Station. Among the objects brought in to be valued are a bracelet with a connection to the Russian royal family, photographs of the Beatles taken during the filming of A Hard Day's Night, and a bronze sculpture by Rodin, which proves a surprise to both the owner and the experts

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: As all paths converge on Cittàgazze, the mission to find Will's father becomes more pressing. Having survived the balloon crash, Jopari and Lee try to locate the knife-bearer, but the Magisterium are in close pursuit. And Ruta updates Serafina on Asriel's plans

programme.title: Sports Personality of the Year
programme.desc: Coverage of the ceremony live from Media City UK in Salford, as the year's key sporting moments are reviewed and the successor to Ben Stokes is crowned following a public vote. The likes of WBC Heavyweight boxing champion Tyson Fury and seven-time Formula One world title-winner Lewis Hamilton are expected to be in contention for the main reward in a year that saw a number of fine performances despite the disruption caused by the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Leeds United and Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City. The two Uniteds were meeting for the first time since a 3-0 League Cup win for the Red Devils at Elland Road in September 2011, while Spurs were looking to emulate their 3-0 victory home win over the Foxes last season. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Aston Villa, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield United and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Everton, Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Manchester City

programme.title: The Big Sick (2017)
programme.desc: A struggling stand-up comedian proves a hit with a student heckler, but their affair ends just before a mystery illness puts the object of his affections in a coma. Finding himself absorbed with Emily's care and bonding with her sparring parents, Kumail also has to struggle with his own Muslim relatives and their expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Kumail Nanjiani, Zoe Kazan, Holly Hunter and Ray Romano

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by cyclist Rachel Atherton, cricketer Ollie Pope, six-time world swimming champion Mark Foster and former footballer Clinton Morrison. Sue Barker asks the questions

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Legend of the NeverBeast (2014)
programme.desc: One of Tinker Bell's friends meets a large hairy monster, and discovers he is not as fearsome as he looks. However, she has a hard time convincing the other fairies to accept him, and needs the help of her friends to protect the creature. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Ginnifer Goodwin and Rosario Dawson

programme.title: The Muppets (2011)
programme.desc: Three fans of the famous puppet variety show discover a powerful businessman is going to knock down the Muppet theatre and drill for oil on the site. They track down frontman Kermit the frog and persuade him to reunite his co-stars, so they can put on one last performance to save the venue. Comedy, starring Jason Segel, Amy Adams and Chris Cooper, with songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat

programme.title: Oz the Great and Powerful (2013)
programme.desc: An amoral circus illusionist is transported to a magical land, where he is mistaken for a powerful wizard prophesied to fall from the sky and save the realm from evil. His attempt to take advantage of the situation leads him into a dangerous quest involving three powerful witches. Fantasy adventure prequel to The Wizard of Oz, starring James Franco, Mila Kunis, Rachel Weisz and Michelle Williams

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: City children Susan and John are spending their summer at Scatterbrook Farm, where they are amazed to discover the scarecrow of Ten Acre Field can come to life. But it seems this extraordinary new friendship has thrown the rhythm of the natural world out of kilter and now they must work to put things right. Mackenzie Crook writes, directs and stars in the first of two new dramas from 2019 based on the books by Barbara Euphan Todd - last adapted 40 years ago in the popular ITV series with Jon Pertwee. Co-starring Steve Pemberton, Rosie Cavaliero and Vicki Pepperdine

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host a children's TV-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Paul Chuckle, Dani Harmer, Gwyneth Powell, George Christopher, Hacker, Dodge, Andy Day and Cerrie Burnell

programme.title: Marcus Rashford: Feeding Britain's Children
programme.desc: A look at the Manchester United and England star's campaign calling for children from poor families in England to get free meals during the school holidays. The 23 year-old striker speaks openly about his personal connection with hunger, his experiences of going without food as a child and the sacrifices his family had to make. The film also hears from those closest to him, including his mum Mel, his brothers Dwaine and Dane and his friends, while Marcus meets some of the families directly affected by the issue

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is living in fear - and it seems he is right to be worried when a loud bang goes off in the Vic, leaving him cowering under a table. Tina is terrified when the police take her down to the station for questioning, Karen is distraught when Gray says he wants the kids for Christmas and Kathy is determined to have all her family together

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A funeral for a pet bird inspires Geraldine to transform the church into an ark and hold a special service for the animals of the village - but David is far from impressed by her stunt. Comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine anounces the winner of the Dibley mask competition, Hugo sets a new record as he delivers a sermon and the vicar reflects on a challenging year as Christmas draws near. The last of three mini-episodes of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French and James Fleet

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef Christmas Cook-Off
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return for the first of two festive specials as they invite memorable celebrities from previous series back to the MasterChef kitchen. Broadcaster and journalist Janet Street-Porter, actor Christopher Biggins, radio presenter Dev Griffin and TV personality Vicky Pattison compete against each other to see who can take home the title of Christmas champion

programme.title: Mission: Impossible (1996)
programme.desc: Secret agents suspect there is a traitor in their organisation when their latest assignment ends in disaster. The sole survivor is accused of being the mole, forcing him to go on the run to clear his name. However, in the process, he discovers a former Russian spy is selling information about the world's leading undercover operatives. Action thriller based on the 1960s TV series, starring Tom Cruise, Jon Voight and Ving Rhames

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: An extended compilation of highlights from the satirical quiz, looking back at the big stories of the year with team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop and a variety of guest hosts and panellists

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to George Clooney about his new sci-fi movie The Midnight Sky. Michael Sheen and David Tennant discuss playing bickering locked-down actors in the second series of Staged, while Oscar-winner Viola Davis talks about American drama Ma Rainey's Black Bottom. Vanessa Kirby reflects on starring in the searing domestic drama Pieces of a Woman and Michael Ball and Alfie Boe take to the stage to perform Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show more clips from the natural world, including an alien-like snail with pulsating antennae and a 10-metre long animal called a pyrosome

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his band of travellers visit a panda reserve in China, learn how kulfi ice-cream is made in India, and pay tribute to the Black Eyed Peas in Luang Prabang

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joshua, 11, and his younger brother Owen are great friends, but Owen has Downs syndrome, which means that a lot of his parents' time is taken up looking after him. Joshua loves DJ'ing and admires Radio 1's Annie Mac. Here, helpers make Joshua his very own DJ den

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: It's our big Christmas special! Expect a studio full of festive fun with a music mash up from 220 Kid and JC Stewart, a huge Christmas medley featuring virtual choirs all over the UK; plus cards and messages from our BP fans. And Mwaksy and Richie take to the sky in a glider to put on a huge firework display

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Natural history film comparing the fortunes of polar bears with grizzlies by following two families in the wild. As the Arctic warms up, the creatures' habitats are beginning to overlap, causing the animals to compete for food and space, and in some cases interbreed. Narrated by Peter Guinness

programme.title: The Greatest Story Ever Told (1965)
programme.desc: The life of Jesus, from His lowly birth in Bethlehem and the miracles He performed, to His ultimate betrayal at the hands of disciple Judas Iscariot and His death by crucifixion. Biblical drama, starring Max von Sydow, David McCallum, Telly Savalas, Charlton Heston, Angela Lansbury and Sidney Poitier, plus several cameos including John Wayne

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Cookbook
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers plan a festive get-together for the people they have met during their TV adventures - but while promising plenty of food and drink, they decide there will be no turkey and trimmings, focusing instead on the best party food they can prepare. So begins a UK-wide search for canapes, mini-desserts, Thai treats and Indian delights, before they build the ultimate centrepiece for the party - a vodka ice luge - and turn their venue into a winter wonderland ready for their guests to arrive

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Days (1956)
programme.desc: Victorian gentleman Phileas Fogg wagers he and his loyal valet Passepartout can circumnavigate the globe in only 80 days. Oscar-winning period adventure, based on Jules Verne's novel, starring David Niven, Cantinflas and Shirley MacLaine, with cameo appearances by stars including Noel Coward, Marlene Dietrich, Frank Sinatra, Buster Keaton and John Gielgud

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: David Harewood narrates a documentary exploring three of the most exotic and remote islands on the planet, beginning with the unique and extraordinary wildlife of Madagascar. As the oldest island on Earth, life has had time to evolve and there are now more unique plants and animals on Madagascar than any other, with footage of ring-tailed lemurs, labord's chameleons and Decken's sifakas

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Cram, Lloyd Griffith, Jeanette Kwakye and Josie Long test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared at the end of today's programme

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch plans to bring a bit of Christmas spirit to Slade Prison in the form of his own home-made booze. But when Mackay and Barrowclough uncover the stash in his and Godber's cell, the crafty con seems in danger of spending the yuletide period in solitary confinement. Festive edition of the classic comedy from 1976, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay

programme.title: The Eagle Has Landed (1976)
programme.desc: A Nazi secret agent plans to kidnap Winston Churchill while the prime minister spends a weekend in the Norfolk countryside, and plants covert operatives in the area ready for his arrival. Second World War adventure, adapted from Jack Higgins' novel, starring Michael Caine, Donald Sutherland, Robert Duvall, Jenny Agutter, Donald Pleasence, Anthony Quayle and Jean Marsh

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The actor reminisces about his movie career, which has spanned more than 60 years, in a series of archive interviews woven together with contributions from colleagues and critics, plus clips from some of his most famous films. They include Zulu, which gave the future knight his first starring role, espionage adventure The Ipcress File, gangster thriller Get Carter and the 2009 film Harry Brown, in which he appeared as a vigilante OAP

programme.title: My Generation (2017)
programme.desc: Michael Caine narrates and stars in his own personal journey through 1960s London. Based on personal accounts and archive footage, this documentary sees Caine travel back through time to talk to the Beatles, Twiggy, David Bailey, Mary Quant, the Rolling Stones, David Hockney and other star names. The film has been assembled over the last six years by Caine working with producer Simon Fuller, writers Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais, and director David Batty, to tell the story of the birth of pop culture in London through the eyes of the young Michael Caine

programme.title: Educating Rita (1983)
programme.desc: A Liverpool hairdresser unhappy with her marriage and her lot in life enrols on an Open University course hoping to expand her horizons. The lively pupil provides some much-needed inspiration for her boozy, world-weary tutor, but over time, he begins to question whether his influence is entirely positive. Comedy drama based on Willy Russell's stage play, starring Michael Caine and Julie Walters

programme.title: Great Expectations (2012)
programme.desc: A penniless orphan is chosen by a reclusive spinster to be a playmate for her cold-hearted ward, whom he falls in love with. As he grows up, a mysterious benefactor offers him the chance to rise into high society, but dark secrets lie behind both the object of his adoration and his newfound fortune. Period drama based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring Jeremy Irvine, Helena Bonham Carter, Ralph Fiennes and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: There's a huge boost to the breeding programme, as an anteater gives birth in the run-up to Christmas. As Kate Humble reviews the footage with the keepers it reveals some worrying behavior from the mum towards her newborn. their stable at night in the winter months. Meanwhile, Ben Fogle contemplates whether reindeer really fly and Jean Johansson gives the lions their Christmas stockings and continues to find out if any animals really like sprouts.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on a quest to find little critters - from bugs and beetles to crabs and worms. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes early lambs to his farm in the Cotswolds, and Tom Heap investigates a tale of two rural high streets in lockdown

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond explores the Christmas traditions of old. Plus, a look back at some highlights from across the year, including Anne Lundon's stunning favourite beach on Lewis and Euan McIlwraith's vintage tractor. The team also pays tribute to the amateur film-makers who kept Landward on air throughout lockdown

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Eagles are the most powerful birds in the sky, capable of tackling enormous prey, spotting food at vast distances and soaring for miles on the wing. Armed with cutting-edge filming technology, this documentary examines the science behind such extraordinary abilities. Featuring dramatic stories and some remarkable experiments, the film follows the story of a family of bald eagles in Iowa as two chicks face the challenges of life in the wild. Plus, bird specialist Lloyd Buck puts his trained golden eagle Tilly's skills to the test

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson travels to Venice to soak up the atmosphere and gain inspiration for dishes for a Christmas party with an Italian twist. Back in the UK, she creates a tasty buffet menu for her guests, preparing a Venetian cocktail served with quick and easy canapes including Parmesan shortbreads and panettone stuffing squares. Nigella's take on the traditional roast bird is turkey breast stuffed with Italian sausage and marsala-steeped cranberries, served with salad and Brussels sprout pasta. Guests are treated to desserts including a marshmallow cappuccino pavlova and a Christmas pudding cake adorned with chocolate chips and pomegranate seeds

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham finds out about the much-loved dolphin that has started breaking people's bones and reveals how a tiny crustacean has mastered the holy grail of science - the art of invisibility

programme.title: Life in the Snow
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan goes in search of animals that live in snowy conditions and have adapted to survive in cold weather. The programme features polar bears raising their cubs, owls keeping their supplies of food hidden under a layer of snow, penguins that huddle together for warmth, and wolverines and ravens working alongside each other to find food. He also takes a look at the lives of animals associated with Christmas, including robins and reindeer, revealing the truth behind the story of Rudolph's red nose

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at the career of the Oscar-winning actor through interviews conducted with the BBC. Hopkins talks about his approach to acting, his years as a hellraiser, and the films that helped make him a star, including Remains of the Day, Shadowlands, Nixon, and The Silence of the Lambs, in which he first portrayed his most celebrated character, Dr Hannibal Lecter. Sylvia Syms narrates

programme.title: Shadowlands (1993)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the later life of novelist CS Lewis, detailing how he fell in love with American poet Joy Gresham, only to see his new-found happiness marred by tragedy and illness. Directed by Richard Attenborough and starring Anthony Hopkins, Debra Winger, Edward Hardwicke, John Wood, Michael Denison and Joseph Mazzello

programme.title: Chris & Michaela - Under the Christmas Sky
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan travel the world to visit areas famous for natural light displays in the sky, discovering the traditions of the local people and going in search of winter wildlife. Their journey takes them to the desert of Jordan, where they get a taste of nomadic Bedouin life, while scanning the skies for a rare planetary conjunction that could be the origin for the story of the Star of Bethlehem. Going to the opposite climatic extreme, they visit Norway's frozen wilderness to see the Northern Lights, snow-mobiling with a Sami family and discovering how eagles and reindeer thrive in icy conditions

programme.title: Catch Me If You Can (2002)
programme.desc: The story of Frank Abagnale Jr, who found notoriety as a teenager conning and charming his way across America. Posing as a pilot, a doctor and a lawyer, Frank was able to fund an extravagant lifestyle while outwitting the FBI at every turn. Fact-based drama, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Tom Hanks, Christopher Walken, Martin Sheen and James Brolin

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: The comedian and explorer revisits his fifth travel series - Himalaya. For Michael Palin, this epic journey across the world's highest mountain range is the fulfilment of boyhood dreams inspired by the 1953 ascent of Everest by Hillary and Tensing. Beginning in the famous Khyber Pass, he travels through Pakistan and India, ascends into Nepal and Tibet to reach Everest base camp itself

programme.title: The Death of Stalin (2017)
programme.desc: The place is Moscow, the year is 1953 and after being in power for nearly 30 years, Soviet dictator Joseph Stalin dies after an inconveniently brief illness. Amid the shock, the members of the Council of Ministers engage in a chaotic bid for power. Armando Iannucci's political satire, starring Andrea Riseborough, Jason Isaacs, Rupert Friend, Simon Russell Beale, Steve Buscemi, Jeffrey Tambor and Michael Palin

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian is in the Canadian Arctic for this festive special of the travelogue in which he explores countries rarely visited by tourists. He wants to know whether there is more to this area than the prospect of freezing to death or being eaten by a polar bear. Travelling across frozen wastelands with host Johnny Issaluk, he spends a week in the largest territory, getting to grips with the Inuit hunter's way of life - not an easy task for vegan Romesh. He also witnesses the erosion and resurgence of Inuit traditions and discover why alcohol is often restricted in the communities

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Armed with a Taser, Claire tries to take down her first target - an escapee who can create black holes. Peter flees from the death and destruction in the future, and realises how much the experience has changed him. Frustrated that his contact with Adam failed to get him closer to the formula, Hiro befriends Daphne and new associate Knox as his quest continues. Meanwhile, Suresh puts Maya in trouble by trying to correct research errors

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Knox and Daphne are impressed by Hiro's ruthlessness and bring him to Pinehearst Industries for an assignment. With Angela and the Company paralysed, Peter seeks answers from the rival organisation, but a meeting with its founder will change him for ever. Claire and her mother try to free Meredith from the clutches of escaped villain Doyle, who can take mental and physical control of others. Nathan and Tracy visit Suresh, hoping he can shed light on the origin of their abilities

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian's commitment to going cold turkey is tested when Sarah Jane calls in a favour, and he finds himself tempted to crack under the pressure. Jodie feels pushed aside when Sacha's daughter starts asking questions about his surprise engagement, but refuses to give up without a fight. Nicky does not know who to believe when Chloe accuses Cameron of abusing his position

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Guest chefs and past series winners Jason Atherton and Tom Aikens join the judging panel, as the contestants get to work on the fish and mains courses. They prepare a salmon and a turbot cooked in a giant antique copper pan, while the main course is a roast combining 12 different birds. The chefs must then judge each other's efforts in a blind taste test

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The top three chefs from the fish and main heats cook for the judges, hoping to secure their place as part of the special festive feast in honour of Britain's key workers. For the fish course, there is salmon and turbot, cooked in a giant antique copper pan known as a turbot kettle. For the main course, dishes include venison and a roast that combines 12 different birds. Hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Nuala spends the morning pregnancy-testing the livestock on her family farm, where her father and brother tend 200 dairy cows. Jennie attends Minnie the Jack Russell, who has delivered one of her pups, with more still to come. However, Minnie's labour has slowed dangerously and Jennie has to act fast if she is to save the lives of the rest of the litter - leading to a difficult decision for owner Jamie. Meanwhile, an emergency case leaves Darren delivering two sets of twins

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: In the final episode, Mary travels to the Highlands. Surrounded by magical scenery, snow-capped mountains and frost covered forests, Mary makes delicious food to warm her up after a day out in the cold, including a sausage and pepper hotpot, simple twice-baked souffles, and an onion and sage open puff pastry tart

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Five pairs of competitors journey around the globe, carrying only limited cash and unable to get on a plane or use mobile phones and credit cards. In the first edition, the teams set off from Mexico City, hoping to reach the first of seven checkpoints - Copan Ruinas in Honduras. Each team adopts a different strategy, with married couple Jen and Rob quickly deciding on a route via Belize, but confusion on the Metro leads to a delay

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of puppies and their owners, including border collie Meg, who has her work cut out for her as she trains to be a sheepdog. Cameras also follow great dane Shelby as he joins a young family which already includes Mr Cooper, a three-year-old dachshund

programme.title: Wild Tales from the Village
programme.desc: Documentary following the wildlife near a remote French village, including red squirrels, wild boar, dormice and stone martens. As the animals struggle to survive the winter, they take full advantage of living so close to unsuspecting humans in their search for food, while trying not to be detected. Narrated by Tcheky Karyo

programme.title: Viva Las Vegas (1964)
programme.desc: A charismatic racing driver arrives in the city to prepare for its first grand prix. Unfortunately, his car has no engine, so to make the money to buy one he takes a job as a casino waiter. However, he loses focus when he falls in love, leaving a rival with the means to steal the race and his girl. Musical, with Elvis Presley, Ann-Margret, Cesare Danova and William Demarest

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross raids the archives for the most memorable buys over the past 20 years, with early appearances from David Dickinson, Tim Wonnacott, Kate Bliss and Phil Serrell

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores some of the most bizarre and extraordinary natural events on the planet. This episode visits the town brought to a standstill by a swarm of flying insects, meets the polar bears whose bizarre friendships shocked a community and the sleepless residents kept awake by a lover's deafening serenade

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago (1965)
programme.desc: A married Russian physician falls in love with another woman, but as their passionate affair plays out against the raging backdrop of the First World War and the Bolshevik revolution, he is torn between his desires and his loyalty to his wife. David Lean's epic drama based on Boris Pasternak's novel, starring Omar Sharif, Julie Christie, Geraldine Chaplin, Rod Steiger, Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Tom Courtenay and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Christmas traditions from three distinct parts of Britain. They begin by joining Advent preparations in the Highlands, meeting a Christmas tree farmer, a candlemaker and a woman who makes a traditional Scottish delicacy

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Festive special from 1975. In the run-up to Christmas, Frank loses yet another job but hopes to make up for it by appearing on a DIY programme - only to blow more than a fuse with his efforts. Undeterred, he sets out to pass his driving test on the 10th attempt, and if speeding up an airport runway is not bad enough, things take a turn for the worse when the examiner steps into the car and discovers there is something fishy about Frank. Michael Crawford and Michele Dotrice star, with a guest appearance by David Jacobs

programme.title: Butterflies
programme.desc: Ria thinks of Christmas as a time of truth - but finds herself having to do everything she hates the most. A 1979 festive special of the vintage comedy by Carla Lane, starring Wendy Craig, Geoffrey Palmer and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey find out how favourite festive party foods are made. Gregg visits an enormous factory in Nottingham where 200,000 canapes are produced every 24 hours. Cherry discovers fail-safe scientific methods for cooking the perfect Christmas roast, turning her oven into a turkey sauna by popping ice cubes into a baking tray. Historian Ruth Goodman looks into the tradition of kissing under the mistletoe and also investigates how the period of advent, traditionally associated with fasting, came to be connected with chocolate-packed calendars

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Lyndon B Johnson, A Song of Ice and Fire, seabirds, and children's TV, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Sliders and the Puzzle Hunters compete for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Andromeda, Fake, Zachariah, and Vendetta

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the academic quiz's festive incarnation, with the opening first-round match seeing Christ's Cambridge take on St John's Oxford. Among those facing questions from host Jeremy Paxman are painter and broadcaster Lachlan Goudie and poet Helen Mort for Christ's, while the St John's team includes BBC business editor Simon Jack and author John Lanchester

programme.title: Upstart Crow
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Gemma Whelan star in a two-hander Christmas special, in which Will and Kate find themselves spending Christmas in lockdown far from their families as the plague ravages London. Kate fills her days in isolation with an endless series of projects, Will has more pressing concerns - with James I now on the throne, he needs to come up with a masterpiece to retain favour at the royal court and avoid execution. But while he may have time on his hands to write, inspiration is in very short supply

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Trevor and Julia always want to make Christmas really special for their family, but this year money is so tight that things are not going to be easy. As each door of the Advent calendar opens, everyone will have to do their bit to make the festive season happen. Darkly comic anthology written by and starring The League of Gentlemen and Psychoville's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with guest appearances by Debbie Rush, Gaby French, Bobby Schofield and Olly Hudson-Croker

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: A special edition of the topical comedy show featuring seasonal material, fond favourites and previously unseen bits, with host Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Revenant (2015)
programme.desc: A frontiersman leading a hunting party through the wilderness is mauled by a bear, and a travelling companion who pledged to stay with him until help comes kills his son and leaves him for dead. Against all odds, the wounded man survives his injuries and embarks on a gruelling quest for revenge that takes him through a harsh landscape. Western, starring an Oscar-winning Leonardo DiCaprio, with Tom Hardy and Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on a quest to find little critters - from bugs and beetles to crabs and worms. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes early lambs to his farm in the Cotswolds, and Tom Heap investigates a tale of two rural high streets in lockdown

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: This winter sees the opening of The Loneliness of the Soul, a new exhibition at London's Royal Academy, showcasing the work of Tracey Emin, alongside paintings by Edvard Munch. Munch's work has long inspired Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental new nine-metre high bronze sculpture, The Mother, set to be installed outside Oslo's new Munch Museum next spring. This documentary charts the affinities between Munch's art and Emin's, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britain's pre-eminent artists

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The contest reaches the semi-finals, in which the remaining 10 chefs must prepare a dish based on their favourite food memory. Five of the chefs will be chosen to go through to the next round, while the remaining five must face an invention test to preserve their place in the contest. This challenge involves preparing a dish for a fine dining food delivery experience in 90 minutes

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Another chance to see Ronnie Barker's final screen appearance, originally aired two months after his death in 2005, in which he and Ronnie Corbett choose their favourite sketches, songs and monologues from their festive shows, including the Milkman's Christmas Message, Christmas Day in the Yukon and an interpretation of Alice Through the Looking Glass. Katie Melua sings Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas, before the duo sign off for the very last time

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bjork and Jack Dee introduce the biggest-selling songs of 1995, plus the Christmas Day Number One - Michael Jackson's Earth Song. Featuring performances by N-Trance, Annie Lennox, Take That, Boyzone, the Outhere Brothers, Simply Red, Robson and Jerome, Pulp and Blur

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent embarks on a trip back in time by setting the remote control for December 1995 and serving an irreverent look back at the festive viewing options that faced the nation

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Christmas special from 1995. The Meldrews' wedding anniversary celebrations turn sour as they learn cousin Ursula has died while watching Noel Edmonds - forcing Victor to defy a witch's grim warning and set out to put her affairs in order

programme.title: Saturday Night Fever - The Ultimate Disco Movie
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the 40th anniversary of the blockbuster dance movie. Bruno Tonioli, a young dancer in New York in 1977, revisit the streets where the film was shot and looks back at the success of a production that gave everyone 'disco fever'. Tonioli and contributors including its star John Travolta, recount how a small-budget film and a quickly assembled soundtrack became a mega-hit that changed popular culture for ever. The programme also hears about the potent influence of impresario Robert Stigwood, whose faith in the young Travolta and then-stuttering pop group the Bee Gees proved visionary

programme.title: Bee Gees at the BBC and Beyond
programme.desc: A compilation of classic performances by the legendary pop trio from the BBC archive. Including renditions of the brothers' most popular hits, 1960s appearances on European television performing I Started a Joke, footage from a Top of the Pops staging of World, and some later concerts from the 1990s

programme.title: Disco at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances of disco classics by acts including Chic, Rose Royce, Labelle, Gladys Knight and Village People, from shows such as Top of the Pops, The Old Grey Whistle Test and Later with Jools Holland

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on May 24, with performances by artists including Beats International, En Vogue, Mantronix featuring Wondress, Michael Bolton, Don Pablo's Animals, Was (Not Was) and the B-52s

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on May 31, with performances by artists including Black Box, Talk Talk, Sam Brown, Erasure, Chad Jackson, England and New Order, the Charlatans and Adamski

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bjork and Jack Dee introduce the biggest-selling songs of 1995, plus the Christmas Day Number One - Michael Jackson's Earth Song. Featuring performances by N-Trance, Annie Lennox, Take That, Boyzone, the Outhere Brothers, Simply Red, Robson and Jerome, Pulp and Blur

programme.title: Mrs Dickens' Family Christmas
programme.desc: Sue Perkins explores Charles Dickens' marriage through the eyes of his wife, Catherine - and exposes the reality of their festive family holiday, which was much different to the heart-warming versions he wrote about in A Christmas Carol. Focusing on the woman behind the man, Sue reveals parallels between the female characters he created and in his changing affections for his other half, explores the realities of Victorian marriage and follows Catherine's recipe for a Twelfth Night cake

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part one of two. Adaptation of Charles Dickens' story. An orphan boy endures a horrible childhood in the workhouse, but yearns for more. After being sold to a cruel undertaker, he runs away to London - where he joins a group of pickpockets. Timothy Spall heads an all-star cast as Fagin, with support from Tom Hardy, Gregor Fisher, Sarah Lancashire, Sophie Okonedo, Julian Rhind-Tutt and William Miller in the title role

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lonely residents of the street find company with each other as Christmas arrives. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Kumars at No 42
programme.desc: Neil Morrissey pops in for a chat and Cliff Richard sings one of his festive floor-fillers as the family decide to stay at home this Christmas instead of visiting their relatives in India. But Sanjay's hopes of a relaxing holiday are soon forgotten when he is made to parade a frightening collection of garish outfits from Christmases past. Sanjeev Bhaskar and Meera Syal star in the comedy chat show

programme.title: Goodness Gracious Me
programme.desc: The Coopers sit down to their Christmas dinner and the people of Hounslow stage their very own cosmopolitan nativity play. Asian comedy sketch show, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar, Meera Syal, Nina Wadia and Kulvinder Ghir with Fiona Allen and Dave Lamb

programme.title: Soul Noel: Gospel & Soul Stars Singing Christmas
programme.desc: Christmas concert from the Porchester Hall in London, featuring festive songs with a gospel and soul twist. Soul singers Beverley Knight, Carleen Anderson and Ola Onabule perform alternative versions of songs including We Wish You a Merry Christmas, Hark! The Herald Angels Sing and Jingle Bells. Plus, performances by the Golden Gate Quartet, which was formed in 1934, young soul singer Bryn Christopher, and the London Community Gospel Choir

programme.title: A Christmas Carol
programme.desc: Simon Callow's one-man performance of the classic Christmas ghost story from London's Arts Theatre, based on the adaptation Dickens created for his public appearances

programme.title: Mrs Dickens' Family Christmas
programme.desc: Sue Perkins explores Charles Dickens' marriage through the eyes of his wife, Catherine - and exposes the reality of their festive family holiday, which was much different to the heart-warming versions he wrote about in A Christmas Carol. Focusing on the woman behind the man, Sue reveals parallels between the female characters he created and in his changing affections for his other half, explores the realities of Victorian marriage and follows Catherine's recipe for a Twelfth Night cake

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part one of two. Adaptation of Charles Dickens' story. An orphan boy endures a horrible childhood in the workhouse, but yearns for more. After being sold to a cruel undertaker, he runs away to London - where he joins a group of pickpockets. Timothy Spall heads an all-star cast as Fagin, with support from Tom Hardy, Gregor Fisher, Sarah Lancashire, Sophie Okonedo, Julian Rhind-Tutt and William Miller in the title role

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker reminisce from behind the famous desk about their all-time favourite sketches, including Home Rule for Ruislip, Crossed Lines and the Phantom Raspberry Blower. Lisa Stansfield provides an interlude to the comic mayhem with a performance of I've Got the World on a String

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a cabin and shed nestled among snowdrifts

programme.title: Charles Dickens and the Invention of Christmas
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones reveals how Charles Dickens virtually invented the idea of a traditional family Christmas through one of his books, A Christmas Carol. When it was published in 1843, the story of Ebenezer Scrooge captured the imagination of Victorian Britain, and boosted the new craze for the festive season at a time when the idea of Santa Claus, mistletoe and crackers were relatively new

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sikes escapes back to London with a badly wounded Oliver, while Mr Bumble declares his love for Mrs Corney - and captivated by the promise of social advancement, she agrees to marry him. Later, Nancy stumbles on a secret meeting, during which she overhears Monks telling Fagin he wants Oliver dead in two days

programme.title: The 12 Drinks of Christmas
programme.desc: Comedy actor and Pointless host Alexander Armstrong and his journalist brother-in-law Giles Coren get together to select 12 different drinks for the festive period. Their choices range from the best Christmas Eve cocktail to the wine to serve with lunch, as well as brandy, punch and the ultimate eggnog

programme.title: How Quizzing Got Cool: TV's Brains of Britain
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell, Judith Keppel, Chris Tarrant, Mark Labbett and Nicholas Parsons help Ben Miller trace the origins of the pro-quizzers; quiz show veterans who have emerged from the crowd of quiz aficionados in recent years to achieve fame and fortune for their general knowledge prowess

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: Mark Kermode celebrates one of the most perennial of all movie genres - the Christmas film. He unwraps a glittering selection of festive cinematic treats, from Hollywood blockbusters to family favourites, revealing the techniques and storytelling secrets that make them so successful. Touching on styles ranging from fairy-tale fantasy to high-octane action, family drama to horror, Mark considers how the best of these films are able to capture something magical - the Christmas spirit. But how do they do it?

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Arthur Sleep hosts a Christmas quiz featuring funny festive clips and a seasonal singalong. Professor Muddles and Ann Teak join team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt as they attempt to win not just Gigglepoints but some special Christmas Gigglepresents too. The first round is a simple clip round with a question for each team. The second round kicks off with Nana Knickerbocker’s Panto Showdown, featuring Nana’s cows in a Christmassy Kitchen attempting to make messy Christmas puddings. Then it's on to the third and final buzzer round, and this is Gail and Keith’s last chance to win their Gigglepresents. They both get ready on their buzzers as Arthur reveals a special previously unseen festive clip, featuring DIY Dan clumsily trying to decorate a Christmas tree. Professor Muddles has a bright idea to end the show on a singalong. And so, the teams gather round to sing us out with a lovely rendition of We Wish You a Giggle Quizmas.

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Arthur Sleep hosts a Christmas quiz featuring funny festive clips and a seasonal singalong. Professor Muddles and Ann Teak join team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt as they attempt to win not just Gigglepoints but some special Christmas Gigglepresents too. The first round is a simple clip round with a question for each team. The second round kicks off with Nana Knickerbocker’s Panto Showdown, featuring Nana’s cows in a Christmassy Kitchen attempting to make messy Christmas puddings. Then it's on to the third and final buzzer round, and this is Gail and Keith’s last chance to win their Gigglepresents. They both get ready on their buzzers as Arthur reveals a special previously unseen festive clip, featuring DIY Dan clumsily trying to decorate a Christmas tree. Professor Muddles has a bright idea to end the show on a singalong. And so, the teams gather round to sing us out with a lovely rendition of We Wish You a Giggle Quizmas.
