programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The colourful characters have fun in the garden, but their trousers keep falling down

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: You are invited to a Christmas school play in this bedtime story, Owl or Pussycat?, read by Justin Fletcher.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Angela Hartnett, and comedian Micky Flanagan. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson makes macaroni cheese with a sauce containing brown crab, gruyere and Aleppo pepper, and prepares chicken with orzo that owes a lot to one of her mother's recipes. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Everton v Chelsea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads to Llandudno to explore the rugged beauty of Great Orme, a giant headland overlooking the Irish Sea. Along the way he visits St Tudno's church and copper mines that date back 4,000 years. His next walk takes him to the remote Berwyn mountains, where he treks from Llangynog to the Pistyll Rhaeadr waterfall

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering the truth about football in Wales. It's a story of weekend warriors, boggy pitches and dodgy tackles, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair in tonight's episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees Bill, HRVY, Jamie and Maisie perform a dance chosen by the judges, their showdance and their favourite routine from the series. The people with the paddles - Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse - will critique each performance, but it will be up to the viewers alone to determine who will be taking the glitterball from reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher. There'll also be a performance by pop superstar Robbie Williams

programme.title: Match of the Day

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present the latest action, including the Thursday night match between Las Vegas Raiders and Los Angeles Chargers

programme.title: Notes on a Scandal (2006)
programme.desc: Lonely history teacher Barbara Covett's acerbic personality means she has few friends. When a spirited woman takes a job in the art department of the school where Barbara works, the elderly lady develops a dark obsession with her new colleague, and the discovery of an affair with a student sets her on a mission to destroy the life of her new 'friend'. Drama, starring Judi Dench, Cate Blanchett, Bill Nighy, Tom Georgeson and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park, Southampton v Manchester City at St Mary's Stadium, Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park and Newcastle United v Fulham at St James' Park

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Cornish Christmas traditions, visiting the cathedral where Nine Lessons and Carols first began - with the intention of getting people out of pubs and into church

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the fourth Sunday of Advent, the Rev Canon Roland Riem leads the morning worship from Winchester Cathedral. With carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a special performance from the boys choir Libera

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: Interviews with the Olympic athlete, in which he discusses his faith, his current work as a coach for Team GB and the failed drugs test that marred the end of his career

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is joined by Cliff Richard, Katherine Jenkins and the Priests at St Paul's Cathedral to reveal the results of a viewers' poll to select the nation's top 10 carols. The winning song will be performed live by opera singer Andrea Bocelli, while a panel of experts including Gareth Malone, Pam Rhodes, Karen Gibson, Mark De-Lisser and Andrew Carwood, explain the fascinating stories and histories behind the songs featured

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Kristin Scott Thomas makes an appeal on behalf of Winston's Wish, a charity providing support for bereaved children and their families

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: The Merthyr Mermaid
programme.desc: Documentary following ice swimmer Cath Pendleton and her dream of swimming a mile in the coldest continent on the planet - Antarctica

programme.title: Land of the Wild: After Dark
programme.desc: Cutting-edge camera technology reveals the secrets of Wales's nocturnal wildlife. Filmed across the seasons, from the biting cold of winter to sweltering summer nights, a variety of innovative cameras capture wild action between sunrise and sunset, from swimming hedgehogs and fishing bats, to super moons and comets. Military-grade long lens thermal cameras, which record heat rather than light, film 20,000 starlings racing to roost under Aberystwyth's pier and bats hunting insects over Margam Lake. Super-sensitive cameras which film at night in colour reveal the nocturnal secrets of badgers, moths, hedgehogs, toads and owls, and infrared light illuminates 300 lesser horseshoe bats leaving their castle roost

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team celebrates Christmas on the Auchlyne estate in Scotland. Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley join laird Emma and her family to help monitor beavers and tame some deer. Matt Baker makes decorations for a special Christmas tree that will help birds survive the winter cold, and John Craven finds out about the ancient tradition of Gaelic psalm singing

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Minehead Railway Station. Among the objects brought in to be valued are a bracelet with a connection to the Russian royal family, photographs of the Beatles taken during the filming of A Hard Day's Night, and a bronze sculpture by Rodin, which proves a surprise to both the owner and the experts

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: As all paths converge on Cittàgazze, the mission to find Will's father becomes more pressing. Having survived the balloon crash, Jopari and Lee try to locate the knife-bearer, but the Magisterium are in close pursuit. And Ruta updates Serafina on Asriel's plans

programme.title: Sports Personality of the Year
programme.desc: Coverage of the ceremony live from Media City UK in Salford, as the year's key sporting moments are reviewed and the successor to Ben Stokes is crowned following a public vote. The likes of WBC Heavyweight boxing champion Tyson Fury and seven-time Formula One world title-winner Lewis Hamilton are expected to be in contention for the main reward in a year that saw a number of fine performances despite the disruption caused by the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Leeds United and Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City. The two Uniteds were meeting for the first time since a 3-0 League Cup win for the Red Devils at Elland Road in September 2011, while Spurs were looking to emulate their 3-0 victory home win over the Foxes last season. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Aston Villa, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield United and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Everton, Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Manchester City

programme.title: The Big Sick (2017)
programme.desc: A struggling stand-up comedian proves a hit with a student heckler, but their affair ends just before a mystery illness puts the object of his affections in a coma. Finding himself absorbed with Emily's care and bonding with her sparring parents, Kumail also has to struggle with his own Muslim relatives and their expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Kumail Nanjiani, Zoe Kazan, Holly Hunter and Ray Romano

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by cyclist Rachel Atherton, cricketer Ollie Pope, six-time world swimming champion Mark Foster and former footballer Clinton Morrison. Sue Barker asks the questions

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Legend of the NeverBeast (2014)
programme.desc: One of Tinker Bell's friends meets a large hairy monster, and discovers he is not as fearsome as he looks. However, she has a hard time convincing the other fairies to accept him, and needs the help of her friends to protect the creature. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Ginnifer Goodwin and Rosario Dawson

programme.title: The Muppets (2011)
programme.desc: Three fans of the famous puppet variety show discover a powerful businessman is going to knock down the Muppet theatre and drill for oil on the site. They track down frontman Kermit the frog and persuade him to reunite his co-stars, so they can put on one last performance to save the venue. Comedy, starring Jason Segel, Amy Adams and Chris Cooper, with songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer provides everyday cooking advice, demonstrating recipes that are uncomplicated and easy to remember

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Oz the Great and Powerful (2013)
programme.desc: An amoral circus illusionist is transported to a magical land, where he is mistaken for a powerful wizard prophesied to fall from the sky and save the realm from evil. His attempt to take advantage of the situation leads him into a dangerous quest involving three powerful witches. Fantasy adventure prequel to The Wizard of Oz, starring James Franco, Mila Kunis, Rachel Weisz and Michelle Williams

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: City children Susan and John are spending their summer at Scatterbrook Farm, where they are amazed to discover the scarecrow of Ten Acre Field can come to life. But it seems this extraordinary new friendship has thrown the rhythm of the natural world out of kilter and now they must work to put things right. Mackenzie Crook writes, directs and stars in the first of two new dramas from 2019 based on the books by Barbara Euphan Todd - last adapted 40 years ago in the popular ITV series with Jon Pertwee. Co-starring Steve Pemberton, Rosie Cavaliero and Vicki Pepperdine

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host a children's TV-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Paul Chuckle, Dani Harmer, Gwyneth Powell, George Christopher, Hacker, Dodge, Andy Day and Cerrie Burnell

programme.title: Marcus Rashford: Feeding Britain's Children
programme.desc: A look at the Manchester United and England star's campaign calling for children from poor families in England to get free meals during the school holidays. The 23 year-old striker speaks openly about his personal connection with hunger, his experiences of going without food as a child and the sacrifices his family had to make. The film also hears from those closest to him, including his mum Mel, his brothers Dwaine and Dane and his friends, while Marcus meets some of the families directly affected by the issue

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is living in fear - and it seems he is right to be worried when a loud bang goes off in the Vic, leaving him cowering under a table. Tina is terrified when the police take her down to the station for questioning, Karen is distraught when Gray says he wants the kids for Christmas and Kathy is determined to have all her family together

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A funeral for a pet bird inspires Geraldine to transform the church into an ark and hold a special service for the animals of the village - but David is far from impressed by her stunt. Comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine anounces the winner of the Dibley mask competition, Hugo sets a new record as he delivers a sermon and the vicar reflects on a challenging year as Christmas draws near. The last of three mini-episodes of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French and James Fleet

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef Christmas Cook-Off
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return for the first of two festive specials as they invite memorable celebrities from previous series back to the MasterChef kitchen. Broadcaster and journalist Janet Street-Porter, actor Christopher Biggins, radio presenter Dev Griffin and TV personality Vicky Pattison compete against each other to see who can take home the title of Christmas champion

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian tries his hand at being a classical musician with the help of the BBC National Orchestra of Wales. As he fumbles his way around the instruments to work out which suits him best, the orchestra reveal that they want him to perform with them at the Last Night of the Proms

programme.title: Mission: Impossible (1996)
programme.desc: Secret agents suspect there is a traitor in their organisation when their latest assignment ends in disaster. The sole survivor is accused of being the mole, forcing him to go on the run to clear his name. However, in the process, he discovers a former Russian spy is selling information about the world's leading undercover operatives. Action thriller based on the 1960s TV series, starring Tom Cruise, Jon Voight and Ving Rhames

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: An extended compilation of highlights from the satirical quiz, looking back at the big stories of the year with team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop and a variety of guest hosts and panellists

programme.desc: Ian is living in fear - and it seems he is right to be worried when a loud bang goes off in the Vic, leaving him cowering under a table. Tina is terrified when the police take her down to the station for questioning, Karen is distraught when Gray says he wants the kids for Christmas and Kathy is determined to have all her family together

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A funeral for a pet bird inspires Geraldine to transform the church into an ark and hold a special service for the animals of the village - but David is far from impressed by her stunt. Comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine anounces the winner of the Dibley mask competition, Hugo sets a new record as he delivers a sermon and the vicar reflects on a challenging year as Christmas draws near. The last of three mini-episodes of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French and James Fleet

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef Christmas Cook-Off
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return for the first of two festive specials as they invite memorable celebrities from previous series back to the MasterChef kitchen. Broadcaster and journalist Janet Street-Porter, actor Christopher Biggins, radio presenter Dev Griffin and TV personality Vicky Pattison compete against each other to see who can take home the title of Christmas champion

programme.title: Mission: Impossible (1996)
programme.desc: Secret agents suspect there is a traitor in their organisation when their latest assignment ends in disaster. The sole survivor is accused of being the mole, forcing him to go on the run to clear his name. However, in the process, he discovers a former Russian spy is selling information about the world's leading undercover operatives. Action thriller based on the 1960s TV series, starring Tom Cruise, Jon Voight and Ving Rhames

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: An extended compilation of highlights from the satirical quiz, looking back at the big stories of the year with team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop and a variety of guest hosts and panellists

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to George Clooney about his new sci-fi movie The Midnight Sky. Michael Sheen and David Tennant discuss playing bickering locked-down actors in the second series of Staged, while Oscar-winner Viola Davis talks about American drama Ma Rainey's Black Bottom. Vanessa Kirby reflects on starring in the searing domestic drama Pieces of a Woman and Michael Ball and Alfie Boe take to the stage to perform Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Angela Hartnett, and comedian Micky Flanagan. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson makes macaroni cheese with a sauce containing brown crab, gruyere and Aleppo pepper, and prepares chicken with orzo that owes a lot to one of her mother's recipes. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Everton v Chelsea

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall looking for two things to save from the clutches of the tip crusher. With the help of artisan Norman Wilkinson, Sarah hopes to turn a profit

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair in tonight's episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Comedian Omid Djalili, reality star Charlotte Crosby, TV presenter Dr Zoe Williams and former England rugby international James Haskell answer questions on the Harry Potter Films, Curb Your Enthusiasm, Nelson Mandela, and Whales, Dolphins and Porpoises

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees Bill, HRVY, Jamie and Maisie perform a dance chosen by the judges, their showdance and their favourite routine from the series. The people with the paddles - Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse - will critique each performance, but it will be up to the viewers alone to determine who will be taking the glitterball from reigning champion Kelvin Fletcher. There'll also be a performance by pop superstar Robbie Williams

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize, guided by tonight's seven celebrities, who include Chris Kamara, Gyles Brandreth and Carol Vorderman

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park, Southampton v Manchester City at St Mary's Stadium, Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park and Newcastle United v Fulham at St James' Park

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present the latest action, including the Thursday night match between Las Vegas Raiders and Los Angeles Chargers

programme.title: Notes on a Scandal (2006)
programme.desc: Lonely history teacher Barbara Covett's acerbic personality means she has few friends. When a spirited woman takes a job in the art department of the school where Barbara works, the elderly lady develops a dark obsession with her new colleague, and the discovery of an affair with a student sets her on a mission to destroy the life of her new 'friend'. Drama, starring Judi Dench, Cate Blanchett, Bill Nighy, Tom Georgeson and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park, Southampton v Manchester City at St Mary's Stadium, Everton v Arsenal at Goodison Park and Newcastle United v Fulham at St James' Park

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Cornish Christmas traditions, visiting the cathedral where Nine Lessons and Carols first began - with the intention of getting people out of pubs and into church

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the fourth Sunday of Advent, the Rev Canon Roland Riem leads the morning worship from Winchester Cathedral. With carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a special performance from the boys choir Libera

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: Interviews with the Olympic athlete, in which he discusses his faith, his current work as a coach for Team GB and the failed drugs test that marred the end of his career

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is joined by Cliff Richard, Katherine Jenkins and the Priests at St Paul's Cathedral to reveal the results of a viewers' poll to select the nation's top 10 carols. The winning song will be performed live by opera singer Andrea Bocelli, while a panel of experts including Gareth Malone, Pam Rhodes, Karen Gibson, Mark De-Lisser and Andrew Carwood, explain the fascinating stories and histories behind the songs featured

programme.title: Community Life
programme.desc: An appeal on behalf of the Welcome Organisation, which provides a range of services to around 1,400 people affected by homelessness across greater Belfast every year

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne calls on help to exercise creative flair to an outdated armchair, while blacksmiths Bex and Dave are tasked with turning old scaffolding props into something special

programme.title: Hammy's Boomerang Adventure
programme.desc: Over the Hedge short spin-off. RJ the raccoon gets to work on producing a nature film, which soon turns out to be an excuse to play a series of pranks on Hammy the squirrel. Featuring the voices of Bruce Willis, Steve Carell and Garry Shandling

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live on the coast, including sea lions working in packs to hunt tuna in the shallows and moray eels that venture out of the water to seek out food on dry rocks. Puffins must brave predators to bring a mouthful of food back to their young and king penguins go without food for a month while they moult. Plus, footage of the bizarre Leaping Blenny, a fish that hates the water

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team celebrates Christmas on the Auchlyne estate in Scotland. Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley join laird Emma and her family to help monitor beavers and tame some deer. Matt Baker makes decorations for a special Christmas tree that will help birds survive the winter cold, and John Craven finds out about the ancient tradition of Gaelic psalm singing

programme.title: Life Is Magic
programme.desc: Highlights from the programme in which illusionist Joel M took his conjuring skills to parties and events around the country, helping to create life-changing celebrations

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: As all paths converge on Cittàgazze, the mission to find Will's father becomes more pressing. Having survived the balloon crash, Jopari and Lee try to locate the knife-bearer, but the Magisterium are in close pursuit. And Ruta updates Serafina on Asriel's plans

programme.title: Sports Personality of the Year
programme.desc: Coverage of the ceremony live from Media City UK in Salford, as the year's key sporting moments are reviewed and the successor to Ben Stokes is crowned following a public vote. The likes of WBC Heavyweight boxing champion Tyson Fury and seven-time Formula One world title-winner Lewis Hamilton are expected to be in contention for the main reward in a year that saw a number of fine performances despite the disruption caused by the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Leeds United and Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City. The two Uniteds were meeting for the first time since a 3-0 League Cup win for the Red Devils at Elland Road in September 2011, while Spurs were looking to emulate their 3-0 victory home win over the Foxes last season. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Aston Villa, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield United and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Everton, Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Manchester City

programme.title: The Big Sick (2017)
programme.desc: A struggling stand-up comedian proves a hit with a student heckler, but their affair ends just before a mystery illness puts the object of his affections in a coma. Finding himself absorbed with Emily's care and bonding with her sparring parents, Kumail also has to struggle with his own Muslim relatives and their expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Kumail Nanjiani, Zoe Kazan, Holly Hunter and Ray Romano

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by cyclist Rachel Atherton, cricketer Ollie Pope, six-time world swimming champion Mark Foster and former footballer Clinton Morrison. Sue Barker asks the questions

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Legend of the NeverBeast (2014)
programme.desc: One of Tinker Bell's friends meets a large hairy monster, and discovers he is not as fearsome as he looks. However, she has a hard time convincing the other fairies to accept him, and needs the help of her friends to protect the creature. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Ginnifer Goodwin and Rosario Dawson

programme.title: The Muppets (2011)
programme.desc: Three fans of the famous puppet variety show discover a powerful businessman is going to knock down the Muppet theatre and drill for oil on the site. They track down frontman Kermit the frog and persuade him to reunite his co-stars, so they can put on one last performance to save the venue. Comedy, starring Jason Segel, Amy Adams and Chris Cooper, with songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Oz the Great and Powerful (2013)
programme.desc: An amoral circus illusionist is transported to a magical land, where he is mistaken for a powerful wizard prophesied to fall from the sky and save the realm from evil. His attempt to take advantage of the situation leads him into a dangerous quest involving three powerful witches. Fantasy adventure prequel to The Wizard of Oz, starring James Franco, Mila Kunis, Rachel Weisz and Michelle Williams

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: City children Susan and John are spending their summer at Scatterbrook Farm, where they are amazed to discover the scarecrow of Ten Acre Field can come to life. But it seems this extraordinary new friendship has thrown the rhythm of the natural world out of kilter and now they must work to put things right. Mackenzie Crook writes, directs and stars in the first of two new dramas from 2019 based on the books by Barbara Euphan Todd - last adapted 40 years ago in the popular ITV series with Jon Pertwee. Co-starring Steve Pemberton, Rosie Cavaliero and Vicki Pepperdine

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host a children's TV-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Paul Chuckle, Dani Harmer, Gwyneth Powell, George Christopher, Hacker, Dodge, Andy Day and Cerrie Burnell

programme.title: Marcus Rashford: Feeding Britain's Children
programme.desc: A look at the Manchester United and England star's campaign calling for children from poor families in England to get free meals during the school holidays. The 23 year-old striker speaks openly about his personal connection with hunger, his experiences of going without food as a child and the sacrifices his family had to make. The film also hears from those closest to him, including his mum Mel, his brothers Dwaine and Dane and his friends, while Marcus meets some of the families directly affected by the issue

programme.title: The Paddy Raff Comedy Show
programme.desc: Sketches from the Belfast stand-up and social-media sensation about the festive mayhem and family traditions unique to Northern Ireland. Malone Road's most talked about resident Nigel reveals some family history and his part in the Christmas Story, while Father Pat hosts a rave in a manger, and Granny Raff announces her naughty list. With guest appearances by Michael Smiley and Bronagh Taggart

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: For brothers Nathan and Jake Carter, Christmas means family, music and craic, and while this year might be a little different they are determined to celebrate the only way they know how - a good old country music-inspired celebration from their Fermanagh home. The musical duo perform a host of their favourite Christmas tunes and introduce some very special guests, including Lisa McHugh, Robert Mizzell and Cliona Hagan

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Digital, Culture, Media and Sport Committee's session on the future of public service broadcasting with evidence from Dame Melanie Dawes, Chief Executive, Ofcom, Kevin Bakhurst, Executive Director of Broadcasting and Online Content, Ofcom, followed by Media and Data Minister John Whittingdale, from Tuesday 15 December

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In this first lecture, recorded with a virtual audience, Mark Carney reflects that whenever he could step back from what felt like daily crisis management, the same deeper issues loomed. What is value? How does the way we assess value both shape our values and constrain our choices? How do the valuations of markets affect the values of our society? Carney argues that society has come to embody the Irish writer Oscar Wilde's old aphorism: 'Knowing the price of everything but the value of nothing.' A version for television of the programme first broadcast on BBC Radio 4 on Wednesday 2 December

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Commons by the Health Secretary, Matt Hancock, updating the chamber on the latest on the Covid-19 pandemic ahead of the Christmas period, from Thursday 17 December

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The US electoral college has spoken, even if outgoing president Donald Trump refuses to hear it. Joe Biden categorically and conclusively won the White House and will be moving in next month. So what now for the enraged incumbent and his most loyal associates? Stephen Sackur interviews Chris Ruddy, CEO and owner of Newsmax, the right-wing media organisation that's tried to ‘outfox' Fox News by being ‘Trumpier' than Trump. Is post-Trump America destined to be paralysed with division?

programme.desc: Proceedings in the House of Commons' second chamber, Westminster Hall, on e-petitions relating to financial rewards for government staff and key workers as well as on the vaccination plans for Covid-19, from Monday 14 December

programme.desc: Foreign correspondents currently posted to London look at events in the UK through outsiders' eyes, and at how the issues of the week are being tackled around the world

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty four hours a day - the latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: 100 Women
programme.desc: For thousands of years, the Inuit people lived off the land. Nomadic hunters, chasing targets depending on the seasons. Now those weather patterns have utterly and irreversibly shifted. Locals say that sea ice used to form in September. Now, it forms in October or as late as November. The Arctic is now warming at least twice as fast as the rest of the globe, according to the Arctic Program Report Card. Climate change is threatening the Inuits’ survival - both physically and mentally. Inuit women are trying to hold their families together through it all. Life on Thin Ice follows three generations of Inuit women; a grandmother, mother and daughter to see the impact that climate change has had on their community. What else have they lost along with the ice?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty four hours a day - the latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Italy’s Sunken City
programme.desc: The ancient city of Baiae was the Las Vegas of the Roman Empire. Over the centuries, it slowly sunk beneath the sea. Filmed before the coronavirus pandemic, Amanda Ruggeri meets the archaeologists and engineers developing some surprising new technologies to protect the underwater site for future generations.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty four hours a day - the latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Click

programme.title: Guatemala's Lost World
programme.desc: The Maya Biosphere Reserve is the largest rainforest north of the Amazon and was once the heart of the Maya civilisation. Amanda Ruggeri explores the new technology that is allowing archaeologists to explore the hundreds of structures still hidden beneath the jungle.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty four hours a day - the latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: 100 Women
programme.desc: For thousands of years, the Inuit people lived off the land. Nomadic hunters, chasing targets depending on the seasons. Now those weather patterns have utterly and irreversibly shifted. Locals say that sea ice used to form in September. Now, it forms in October or as late as November. The Arctic is now warming at least twice as fast as the rest of the globe, according to the Arctic Program Report Card. Climate change is threatening the Inuits’ survival - both physically and mentally. Inuit women are trying to hold their families together through it all. Life on Thin Ice follows three generations of Inuit women; a grandmother, mother and daughter to see the impact that climate change has had on their community. What else have they lost along with the ice?

programme.desc: Twenty four hours a day - the latest national and international stories as they break

programme.desc: The Maya Biosphere Reserve is the largest rainforest north of the Amazon and was once the heart of the Maya civilisation. Amanda Ruggeri explores the new technology that is allowing archaeologists to explore the hundreds of structures still hidden beneath the jungle.

programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Plus, the Glasgow Gaelic Music Association sing AlleLuia, Catriona Watt performs Psalm 94, Innes MacSween shares the role faith plays in her life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: Eòrpa (European Current Affairs)
programme.desc: More than four years since the United Kingdom voted to leave the European Union, the Brexit transition period expires at the end of this year. But what preparation have Scottish companies undertaken to ensure they can continue to trade with Europe in the new year? Andrew MacKinnon speaks to a number of businesses and industries to hear their thoughts on Brexit, and to learn about both the opportunities and challenges they see ahead of them

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod visits the Outer Hebrides, where he climbs Harris's highest hill and visits the desolate island of Scarp as he concludes his investigation into the history of place names in the Highlands

programme.title: Beethoven: A’ Chuirm Ghàidhlig
programme.desc: When piper Allan MacDonald revealed Beethoven's song Enchantress, Farewell was a reworking of Gaelic song Mnathan á Ghlinne Seo, German Gaelic-learner Michael Klevenhaus turned detective. We follow his quest to discover more works by the maestro based on traditional music of Gaeldom. Michael reveals that the melodies' origins were deliberately hidden from Beethoven and the public by Edinburgh publisher George Thomson, who commissioned the arrangements from the composer. With the 1745 Jacobite Rebellion still within living memory, Thomson wanted to strip the Gaelic songs of their titles, lyrics and the ‘taint' of Jacobitism. Discovering a wealth of Beethoven's forgotten Gaelic songs, Michael and Allan MacDonald stage a concert featuring both Beethoven's and the ancient Gaelic versions

programme.title: Beethoven: A’ Chuirm Ghàidhlig
programme.desc: The discovery that Beethoven's arrangement of the song Enchantress Farewell was a reworking of a Gaelic song, Mhnathan a' Ghlinne Seo, led German scholar Michael Klevenhaus to search for more connections between old Gaelic melodies and the great German composer. That search featured in the recent documentary Òrain Ghàidhlig Beethoven (Beethoven's Gaelic Songs). This concert, featuring both traditional and classical musicians, highlights more of the Gaelic songs that Michael discovered had found their way to Beethoven and the classical repertoire. The featured musicians are: Traditional singers Mairi MacInnes and Michael Klevenhaus. Scots piper Allan MacDonald and German piper Thomas Zöllner. Scottish classical singers Lorna Anderson (soprano) and Jamie MacDougall (tenor). And the classical instrumentalists Verena Stourzh, Franz Ortner, Clemens Zeilinger of the Trio Van Beethoven

programme.title: Slainte Mhath!
programme.desc: Part two of two. Angela MacEachan hosts a music show featuring songs that praise whisky. Among the performers are Mary Ann Kennedy, Rona Lightfoot, Sineag MacIntyre, Angus MacLeod, Eilidh and Ruairidh Cormack and the vocal group Trosg

programme.desc: Sweeny is excited to see his tweed and reflects on the last 12 months, while also taking a look towards the future

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: A refreshingly honest depiction of the highs and lows of lockdown from all walks of life, some of whom have first hand experience of the virus, and some who are frontline workers

programme.title: Na Tuinichean Mi-thoilichte
programme.desc: Two freshly arrived Scottish settlers in 1780s Nova Scotia are in for a big surprise in their new home and turn to the fast-talking land agent for help

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis invites musicians from around the world to perform from their own homes

programme.title: Moulin Rouge Ecosse
programme.desc: Following three Scottish girls who perform at The Moulin Rouge, Paris in its 130th year

programme.title: FUNC
programme.desc: Trump upsets the locals and Siobhan MacInnes hosts the lottery in the Gaelic comedy sketch show

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz rehearses a dance number that'll make him a star. But when Weasel catapults him next door onto the roof, Magoo thinks he's a stray alien

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel set up their super-antenna in the desert. Magoo ends up beside them. He mistakes the antenna for a forest's last oak tree.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Hidden inside a robot, Fizz and Weasel hack the world's Internet server, but Magoo, shopping the sales, mistakes it for a shopping cart and takes it.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Gordon is dispatched by International Rescue to save two documentary film-makers at risk of falling foul of a mysterious valley filled with strange creatures. Action adventure, with the voice of David Menkin

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: Danny has to try to empty a paddling pool full of balls without using his hands.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean crashes his mini into a pool of slurry and is desperate to get himself and his car - and poor Teddy - clean again!

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: It's Irma's birthday. Bean writes out two cards - one nice, one silly. He gets his envelopes mixed up and sends off the wrong card.

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: How
programme.desc: Sam, Frankie, Vick and Fred pose all-important questions beginning with the word How. Frankie and Vick discover how to pinch a piece of pizza, Doctor HOW shows Sam how to see a song, and Fred's gone potty as he asks how you throw a pot...

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James' countdown to Christmas continues as he's back in the kitchen with First Dates star Fred Siriex, and chefs Clare Smyth and Michael Caines. Plus, there is a Masterclass in edible Christmas decorations and Fred will be taste-testing the best value-for-money alternatives to champagne

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John and Lisa offer up a super simple menu for ‘the big day' in their Christmas Kitchen, starting with a delicious breakfast - a cheesy croissant bake served with a cherry bellini. That is followed by John's crab cocktail, a Christmas dinner traybake, and Christmas pudding parcels and cream

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef enjoys a trip to Southfork, the ranch made famous in TV soap Dallas. While in town, James visits the location of the Texas State Fair and shares his own pastiche of the world-famous drama series

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass

programme.title: Minions (2015)
programme.desc: A race of strange, babbling yellow creatures has served the cause of evil throughout the history of the world. In the 1960s, three of these eternal henchmen get a job working for a criminal genius who is hatching a plot against the British crown - but while carrying out her orders, they uncover a threat to their whole species. Animated comedy, with the voices of Sandra Bullock, Pierre Coffin, Jon Hamm and Michael Keaton

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts a one-off special going behind the scenes of TV's most surreal singing show. The programme features previously unseen interviews and highlights of the series that turned the nation into couch detectives at the start of 2020. Disguised in extravagant costumes including a duck, hedgehog and octopus, 12 celebrities took to the stage and competed to stay anonymous. Set with the task of identifying the Masked Singers were the panel of celebrity detectives - Ken Jeong, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Jonathan Ross

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Manager Rylan Clark-Neal invites Diversity's Jordan Banjo and Perri Kiely, pop icon Martin Kemp and DJ son Roman, and Gavin and Stacey stars Joanna Page and Melanie Walters into his supermarket for the chance to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a festive episode in which celebrity contestants presenter Kate Thornton, reality star James Argent, comedian Lucy Porter and presenter Nik Speakman pit their wits against all five Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams unwraps a batch of brand-new slip-ups for Christmas and reviews the greatest TV blunders of 2020, featuring some of the UK's most-famous faces. David reveals which world leaders and members of the royal family have all been caught out on camera

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes WBC and Ring Magazine World Heavyweight Champion Tyson Fury, who talks about a potential super-fight with fellow Brit and holder of three world titles Anthony Joshua. Also appearing are comedian Russell Kane, actress Joan Collins, TV presenter Maya Jama and multi-platinum musician Jamie Cullum, who chats and performs in the studio

programme.title: The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies (2014)
programme.desc: Dwarf king Thorin reclaims control of his homeland, but his descent into madness places him on the brink of war with his human and elven allies. As Thorin's Hobbit friend Bilbo tries to save the tormented ruler from himself, a vast horde of orcs gathers to destroy them all. Conclusion of the fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Martin Freeman, Richard Armitage, Ian McKellen, Luke Evans and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Having made it through the quarter-finals, two families go head to head in the second semi-final of the tournament, hosted by Anne Hegerty, aiming to move a step closer to winning the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Family and a holiday of a lifetime. Hosted by Anne Hegerty

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, a Christmas volunteer in a supermarket, thinks a burglar is entering overnight and decides to stop him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo forgets to include Mr Cat in his letter to Santa! To make amends he follows Weasel, dressed as an elf, who leads him to the AeroFizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Animation following the kindly but accident-prone Mr Magoo. Magoo mistakes Weasel for a tuna being pursued by a fisherman.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Virgil is dispatched to make a rescue when a sandstorm strikes, but when he too is grounded, only Grandma Tracy is available to take charge of Thunderbird 2 and save the day

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean breaks his bed using it as a trampoline. He tries to fix it, but he can't. Next morning, he goes to buy a brand-new bed.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs. Wicket demands Mr. Bean drive her and Miss Wince to the spa, and wait outside until they're done. But Bean gets bored, and looks for fun!

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series serving as a prequel to the popular film series. Mavis and Uncle Gene go on a road trip together for the very last time.

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Supergirl discovers she needs more than just brawn to stop brainy Catwoman from stealing valuable gems.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: From a location hidden beneath the Pennines, Julia sets off from the Cumbrian village of Dufton in search of one of Britain's best-kept secrets High Cup Nick, the so-called 'Grand Canyon of the North', a hanging glaciated valley 500 million years in the making. Along the route, she meets a family of Olympic rake makers, learns about the notorious Helm wind and meets Mike Hartley, the current record holder of the Pennine Way, who ran the 268 mile-trek in under three days

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Charlie Stemp provide the festive cheer with Alan Titchmarsh, and Collabro perform a Christmas classic acapella on the farm

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ria Hebden and Alex Beresford visit the country's most fascinating attractions sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin witnesses one of the country's greatest natural wonders as she takes a trip down the River Severn during one of its massive tidal bores - and meets the surfers who say there is nothing quite like it. Ben Fogle takes his son to Cornwall, where the pair enjoy some rock pooling and six-year-old Ludo gets the chance to learn to sail. Plus, the programme returns to the farm of shepherdess Amanda Owen in the Yorkshire Dales - where she and her large family are preparing for the annual country show

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John and Lisa offer up a super simple menu for ‘the big day' in their Christmas Kitchen, starting with a delicious breakfast - a cheesy croissant bake served with a cherry bellini. That is followed by John's crab cocktail, a Christmas dinner traybake, and Christmas pudding parcels and cream

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett

programme.title: Diamonds Are Forever (1971)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates a diamond-smuggling operation, and discovers that behind the scheme is his insidious arch-enemy Blofeld, who is planning to construct a devastating orbital doomsday weapon powered by the precious stones, which will force world powers to disarm and give him global domination. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Jill St John, Charles Gray, Bernard Lee and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: A look back at the 2006 win of Ingram Wilcox as he relives his most memorable moments from the game that changed his life. Plus, another chance to see Paul McCartney, George Michael and Ronan Keating on the show

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Back in 2011, McFly singer Tom Fletcher faced the Cube, while his bandmates watched on. Now it's Danny Jones and Dougie Poynter's turn. Can they beat Tom's impressive winnings of £100,000? They're not the only ones appearing tonight, however. Actress and comedian Emily Atack and her mum, the impressionist Kate Robbins, also tackle the various tasks of mental agility and physical ability in a bid to win a quarter of a million for their chosen charities. Presented by Phillip Schofield

programme.title: Bradley Walsh's How to Win a Game Show
programme.desc: The Chase host provides an insight into how to triumph on a TV quiz, featuring classic archive clips. Bradley rounds up a gathering of his fellow presenters, including Ant and Dec, who explain how lying can sometimes pay off, and Stephen Mulhern, who describes the mindset needed to beat Catchphrase. Also dropping in are Bradley's supporting cast of Chasers, who reveal how they prepare for an episode

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: Road Trip
programme.desc: After last year's trip to the unlikely festive destination of Morocco, Gordon Ramsay is determined to celebrate Christmas properly this year, arranging a trip to the ultimate winter wonderland of Lapland. Gino D'Acampo has a letter he wants to hand deliver to Santa from his daughter Mia, Fred is keen to witness the once-in-a-lifetime wonder of the Northern Lights and Gordon is eager that they all encounter the culinary delights of the country. But along the way, they face plenty of distractions, including ice swimming, saunas, snowmobiles and snowballing

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes WBC and Ring Magazine World Heavyweight Champion Tyson Fury, who talks about a potential super-fight with fellow Brit and holder of three world titles Anthony Joshua. Also appearing are comedian Russell Kane, TV presenter Maya Jama and multi-platinum musician Jamie Cullum, who chats and performs in the studio

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: John Neil presents action from Thruxton, featuring round 15 of the British F4 Championship and Round 10 of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actor Jamie Foxx chats with Ross King in Los Angeles about starring in Pixar animated comedy adventure Soul. Lorraine Kelly presents

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a star-studded edition of the game show, with contestants Scarlett Moffatt, Jacqueline Jossa, John Barnes, David Seaman, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph racing down the aisles to win a £10,000 jackpot for the charity of their choice

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: In this edition, Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from Swansea, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the big finale game

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's a time of joy and happiness in Midsomer for once - Christmas is coming, and DCI Barnaby and his wife Sarah are soon to become parents. But the festive mood changes when a man is stabbed to death with an antique sword during a ghost-hunting party at a spooky manor house in Morton Shallows. Is blacksmith's daughter Rose Wilton seeking revenge from beyond the grave, or will Barnaby and sidekick Charlie Nelson find a more earthly motive behind the murder? Starring Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee, with Fiona Dolman, Les Dennis, Elizabeth Berrington, Mark Heap, Emily Joyce, James Murray and Hannah Tointon

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson presents highlights from the show, including Cheryl Hakeney bidding for some Shelley china and £10 junk shop bargain doing well at auction to everyone's surprise

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts as a celebrity team from the world of politics take on the Tenable Tower. Ann Widdecombe, Edwina Currie, John Sergeant, George Galloway and Lembit Opik must join forces as they attempt to win £125,000 for their charities

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites celebrities to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants Julian Clary, Sara Cox and Alan Halsall answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a special festive episode in which celebrity contestants Patti Clare, Matt Richardson, Kirsty Wark and Jimmy Osmond pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chas has regrets, Paul is concerned and Jamie returns from his trip

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick learns that Sam is moving to London with Natasha, and Shona's actions leave David at Ray's mercy. Elsewhere, Paul agrees to meet a distressed Will, and a police officer approaches Sarah and questions her about Rick's watch

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by guest Jeremy Pang to re-create takeaway dishes. The host begins with the Caribbean classic of jerk pork belly with roti and coconut and mango slaw, then rounds things off with a trip to India, with his take on a cauliflower, butternut and spinach dhansak. Meanwhile, Jeremy rustles up a Chinese dish of crispy chilli beef

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adamant that he doesn't want to move to London, Sam begs Nick to let him move in with him and Leanne. Todd covers for Paul as he deceives Billy, and David reaches a sad decision regarding Shona

programme.title: The Big Quiz
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special edition of the quiz show in which teams from ITV's biggest soaps battle it out. Jack P Shepherd captains the Coronation Street team and is joined by Simon Gregson and Sair Khan, while Mark Charnock takes the lead for Emmerdale, alongside Karen Blick and Jay Kontzle

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary following life in the glamorous London hotel over the festive season, as staff prepare to welcome guests during the hotel's most profitable time of the year. Head butler Sean braces new recruit Michael for how busy he is going to be, while restaurant director Anne prepares the four restaurants and her 160 staff. Cameras also follow regular guest Howell Harris as he spends his first Christmas at the hotel since his mother died, with staff going above and beyond to make sure he has a holiday to remember

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Globetrotting Emma is house hunting for love with the help of her cousin Kelly - and she has a substantial list of demands to be met as she visits three single men's homes. In the running is a rugby player, a trendy musician and a bodybuilder. Which one will she choose to go back to her house for a date?

programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Charlie Stemp provide the festive cheer with Alan Titchmarsh, and Collabro perform a Christmas classic acapella on the farm

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ria Hebden and Alex Beresford visit the country's most fascinating attractions sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin witnesses one of the country's greatest natural wonders as she takes a trip down the River Severn during one of its massive tidal bores - and meets the surfers who say there is nothing quite like it. Ben Fogle takes his son to Cornwall, where the pair enjoy some rock pooling and six-year-old Ludo gets the chance to learn to sail. Plus, the programme returns to the farm of shepherdess Amanda Owen in the Yorkshire Dales - where she and her large family are preparing for the annual country show

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John and Lisa offer up a super simple menu for ‘the big day' in their Christmas Kitchen, starting with a delicious breakfast - a cheesy croissant bake served with a cherry bellini. That is followed by John's crab cocktail, a Christmas dinner traybake, and Christmas pudding parcels and cream

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett

programme.title: Diamonds Are Forever (1971)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates a diamond-smuggling operation, and discovers that behind the scheme is his insidious arch-enemy Blofeld, who is planning to construct a devastating orbital doomsday weapon powered by the precious stones, which will force world powers to disarm and give him global domination. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Jill St John, Charles Gray, Bernard Lee and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: A look back at the 2006 win of Ingram Wilcox as he relives his most memorable moments from the game that changed his life. Plus, another chance to see Paul McCartney, George Michael and Ronan Keating on the show

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Back in 2011, McFly singer Tom Fletcher faced the Cube, while his bandmates watched on. Now it's Danny Jones and Dougie Poynter's turn. Can they beat Tom's impressive winnings of £100,000? They're not the only ones appearing tonight, however. Actress and comedian Emily Atack and her mum, the impressionist Kate Robbins, also tackle the various tasks of mental agility and physical ability in a bid to win a quarter of a million for their chosen charities. Presented by Phillip Schofield

programme.title: Bradley Walsh's How to Win a Game Show
programme.desc: The Chase host provides an insight into how to triumph on a TV quiz, featuring classic archive clips. Bradley rounds up a gathering of his fellow presenters, including Ant and Dec, who explain how lying can sometimes pay off, and Stephen Mulhern, who describes the mindset needed to beat Catchphrase. Also dropping in are Bradley's supporting cast of Chasers, who reveal how they prepare for an episode

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: Road Trip
programme.desc: After last year's trip to the unlikely festive destination of Morocco, Gordon Ramsay is determined to celebrate Christmas properly this year, arranging a trip to the ultimate winter wonderland of Lapland. Gino D'Acampo has a letter he wants to hand deliver to Santa from his daughter Mia, Fred is keen to witness the once-in-a-lifetime wonder of the Northern Lights and Gordon is eager that they all encounter the culinary delights of the country. But along the way, they face plenty of distractions, including ice swimming, saunas, snowmobiles and snowballing

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes WBC and Ring Magazine World Heavyweight Champion Tyson Fury, who talks about a potential super-fight with fellow Brit and holder of three world titles Anthony Joshua. Also appearing are comedian Russell Kane, TV presenter Maya Jama and multi-platinum musician Jamie Cullum, who chats and performs in the studio

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: John Neil presents action from Thruxton, featuring round 15 of the British F4 Championship and Round 10 of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's a time of joy and happiness in Midsomer for once - Christmas is coming, and DCI Barnaby and his wife Sarah are soon to become parents. But the festive mood changes when a man is stabbed to death with an antique sword during a ghost-hunting party at a spooky manor house in Morton Shallows. Is blacksmith's daughter Rose Wilton seeking revenge from beyond the grave, or will Barnaby and sidekick Charlie Nelson find a more earthly motive behind the murder? Starring Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee, with Fiona Dolman, Les Dennis, Elizabeth Berrington, Mark Heap, Emily Joyce, James Murray and Hannah Tointon

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites celebrities to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants Julian Clary, Sara Cox and Alan Halsall answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a special festive episode in which celebrity contestants Patti Clare, Matt Richardson, Kirsty Wark and Jimmy Osmond pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adamant that he doesn't want to move to London, Sam begs Nick to let him move in with him and Leanne. Todd covers for Paul as he deceives Billy, and David reaches a sad decision regarding Shona
