Unfortunately, the other winners each face a grim fate when they start to misbehave. Tim Burton's children's fantasy, based on Roald Dahl's novel, starring Johnny Depp, Freddie Highmore, Helena Bonham Carter, and Deep Roy playing all the Oompa Loompas

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (2005)
programme.desc: Penniless Charlie Bucket is one of five lucky children who find a golden ticket in a chocolate bar, winning a once-in-a-lifetime tour of eccentric recluse Willy Wonka's magical sweet company. Unfortunately, the other winners each face a grim fate when they start to misbehave. Tim Burton's children's fantasy, based on Roald Dahl's novel, starring Johnny Depp, Freddie Highmore, Helena Bonham Carter, and Deep Roy playing all the Oompa Loompas

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: All Lois wants is help with the Christmas shopping, but when the family fail her she walks out on them, leaving the remaining Griffins to try to save the holiday season

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger helps Francine with her terrible cooking by bringing her to a remote Patagonian island. Stan gets a nail in his head and can't speak.

programme.title: Crossing Swords
programme.desc: Princess Blossom is kidnapped by a masked assassin, so King Merriman offers a million dollars to whoever can rescue her from the Minotaur's maze

programme.title: Crossing Swords
programme.desc: With the castle under siege from the masked assassin, Patrick must stop the coup and save the royal family and the kingdom

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve looks after Roger's ex-tumour Rogu to prove to Francine that he is ready for the responsibility of babysitting

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A selfish Peter loses his festive spirit and is visited by the ghosts of Christmas past, present and future, who take him on a journey around Quahog

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire lands free plane tickets for a guys' trip to San Francisco, but his friends begin to doubt his flying abilities after getting a look at the autopilot function

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Professional American football player Rob Gronkowski moves into the neighbourhood, while Stewie uses steroids to increase his new bees' honey output, but it makes them aggressive

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan returns home from another dangerous mission to find Francine and the family have decorated the house for Christmas. Deciding their efforts do not live up to his high standards, he sets out to find the perfect tree, but meets an untimely end during his search, and battles the forces of evil to save his loved ones and earn another chance at life

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine gets a job at Stan's office, which leaves him unable to flirt with his co-workers - and to make matters even worse, jealousy leads to an awful encounter

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Lyndsey starts looking for a new apartment and asks Alan to move in with her, but he wonders about leaving the beach house behind. Meanwhile, Walden breaks up with Ms McMartin. Starring Jon Cryer and Courtney Thorne-Smith

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden decides he wants to get divorced from Alan and move on, but to ease the pain, he allows Alan to have one wish. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: American Pie (1999)
programme.desc: Four teenage boys make a solemn pact to lose their virginity before the high-school prom, and though their antics cause them huge embarrassment and public humiliation, the friends also learn about love and affection in their relations with the opposite sex. Coming-of-age comedy, starring Jason Biggs, Shannon Elizabeth, Chris Klein, Alyson Hannigan, Mena Suvari, Seann William Scott and Eugene Levy

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: American Pie (1999)
programme.desc: Four teenage boys make a solemn pact to lose their virginity before the high-school prom, and though their antics cause them huge embarrassment and public humiliation, the friends also learn about love and affection in their relations with the opposite sex. Coming-of-age comedy, starring Jason Biggs, Shannon Elizabeth, Chris Klein, Alyson Hannigan, Mena Suvari, Seann William Scott and Eugene Levy

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to win the affections of Dom, with the buyer of the clothing picked out by the former goth going on a date with him

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Faye announces that she has quit her job, Debbie asks Gary to reconsider his position, and Todd goes snooping in Paul and Billy's flat

programme.title: Carry On Camping (1969)
programme.desc: Two men lure their unsuspecting girlfriends to a nudist camp, with hopes of having a wild old time - but the joke is on the men when it turns out they have ended up at a traditional family campsite. Bawdy comedy, starring Sid James, Bernard Bresslaw, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims and Barbara Windsor

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At a Christmas family get-together, a cracker riddle states two people will die before midnight on Boxing Day, and just a few hours later family matriarch Lydia meets an untimely end - giving Barnaby the perfect reason to escape a boring Christmas with his in-laws. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Margery Mason

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama starring Brenda Blethyn. The detective investigates a fire that rips through a holiday park on the Northumberland coast, destroying three cabins. A young woman's body is discovered and the resort's owner, Jim Viner, fears the victim is his sister Deena when he returns from a weekend away with his daughter Clare. With Jim's suspicions confirmed, a police report then reveals Clare's boyfriend Ryan and his friends had been partying near the scene hours before the blaze started, but where are they now, and where is Deena's best friend Sally? Co-starring Kenny Doughty, Cush Jumbo and Jon Morrison

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective discovers his favourite 1970s band are on the verge of a comeback - along with their singer Esme Ford, who has made a shocking reappearance from beyond the grave, having faked her own death 35 years earlier. But when an orphan is found dead on the other side of town, a forensic link leads Lewis and Hathaway to suspect the aged rockers are somehow involved. Joanna Lumley, David Hayman, Simon Callow and Helen Baxendale guest star alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Faye announces that she has quit her job, Debbie asks Gary to reconsider his position, and Todd goes snooping in Paul and Billy's flat

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel and Jai speak to the kids. Tracy has a plan to reunite Leyla and Liam. Wendy is delighted by Victoria's offer, but Bob is disappointed

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper detective accepts an invitation to spend yuletide at the house of an elderly millionaire who seems intent on provoking his money-grubbing sons into murdering him. At first he tries hard to ignore the strained atmosphere and relax - but soon finds himself with a gruesome mystery on his hands. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Anne: The Princess Royal at 70
programme.desc: A special documentary marking Princess Anne's 70th birthday, as she talks openly about her life and work, and the reality of growing up as a member of the Royal family. Throughout the programme, Princess Anne talks frankly about a family in which women tended to be seen as 'honorary men', about the challenges for younger royalty, about a bloody kidnap attempt, and about refusing titles for her children, Peter and Zara

programme.title: The Life & Times of Captain Sir Tom
programme.desc: A profile of the former British Army officer, who captured the hearts of the nation with his fundraising walk for the NHS at the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. With access to Tom's everyday life, the film explores the man behind the blazer and hears his story, including his time on the frontline during the Burma campaign in the Second World War. Tom also recalls his teenage years, previous jobs and nursing his wife through illness - as well as a near-fatal accident he suffered just 18 months ago

programme.title: Dad's Army (2016)
programme.desc: The officious captain of a seaside town's home guard platoon tries to maintain the reputation and morale of his troops as the Second World War draws to a close. He finds himself dealing with the hunt for a German spy as well as a female journalist sent to report on the exploits of the brave but bumbling soldiers. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Toby Jones, Bill Nighy and Catherine Zeta-Jones

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: The clinic is pushed to its limits when a building collapses in Barco and the doctors face a desperate struggle to save the injured survivors. Ruby's objectivity is put to the test when she discovers a secret concerning one of her patients, and Ram helps a woman whose husband is missing

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: The Royle Family's Sue Johnston guest stars in the final episode of the current series, which sees the staff of the hospital coming together to celebrate Ram and Mala's wedding. Ruby and Gabriel intervene in a dispute that has erupted between two fishermen, while a heart to heart at the wedding ceremony brings the two doctors closer together. Meanwhile, Lydia is confronted with a difficult dilemma when she receives a visit from her former mentor, who has come to the hospital seeking her help

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's a time of joy and happiness in Midsomer for once - Christmas is coming, and DCI Barnaby and his wife Sarah are soon to become parents. But the festive mood changes when a man is stabbed to death with an antique sword during a ghost-hunting party at a spooky manor house in Morton Shallows. Local historian Brendan Pearce thinks blacksmith's daughter Rose Wilton is seeking revenge from beyond the grave, but Barnaby and sidekick Charlie Nelson try to find a more earthly motive behind the murder. Starring Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee, with Fiona Dolman, Les Dennis, Elizabeth Berrington, Mark Heap, Emily Joyce, James Murray and Hannah Tointon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the dramatic and beautiful landscape of the Lake District. Along the way he meets the man who made the world's longest Cumberland sausage, attempts to speed along the steepest road in Britain, crosses the Infinity Bridge and tries to break the record for the slowest water ski in history

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel and Jai speak to the kids. Tracy has a plan to reunite Leyla and Liam. Wendy is delighted by Victoria's offer, but Bob is disappointed

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz entertains Maureen and Deirdre at Singles' Night at the Warwick. Gary rows with Judy for giving Zoe their money. Angie makes a pass at Chris

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen is alarmed when Fred keeps calling her. Deirdre is thrilled when Jon asks her out. Kevin is stunned when Natalie tells him she loves him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak takes advantage of Marlon being in charge at the wine bar. The Cairns family load up the car ready to leave for Germany. Alan tries to talk Eric out of his depression

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is furious about the mess the Dingles have caused at the tearooms. Steve is relieved when Kim agrees to sign his contract. Scott tells Lyn he's not happy in the army

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of bikers wreaks havoc in Aidensfield, prompting the locals to demand a new bobby. When young recruit PC Joe Mason (Joe McFadden) arrives he is not quite what they expected, with his tactics for crime fighting making his reception a mixed one between both residents and fellow police officers

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid takes a little exercise and Trevor decides to join him. Seventies sitcom, starring Sid James and Anthony Jackson

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park Cricket Club prepare for the opening ceremony of their new pitch and pavilion, Mim arranges a big surprise for Rog - but disaster is waiting in the wings. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: On the Buses (1971)
programme.desc: Wily bus driver Stan Butler thinks a new company policy that allows women behind the wheel will rob him of his precious overtime money. Enlisting the aid of conductor Jack, he does everything he can to sabotage the plan. Comedy, based on the TV series, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant, Stephen Lewis, Doris Hare, Peter Madden and Anna Karen

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A teenager arrives in the village asking for directions to Ashfordly Hall - and claims to be looking for her father. David decides to make a will, but becomes paranoid when one of the beneficiaries seems too eager to inherit, and a series of accidents befall him. Elsewhere, a new arrival at the Fossett farm is named after Joe. Guest starring Ophelia Lovibond

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan receives the injunction from Kim's solicitors. Zak embarks on another money-making scheme. Marlon is approached by the police for serving alcohol to under-age children

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe refuses to be bullied by Kim and Steve into giving Kelly a job. Emma insists that she does not want to go back to Germany. Tara confronts Steve about his dad

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin and Natalie plan to spend the night together. The McDonalds go to pick up Steve when he is released from prison. Alma tells Curly she still wants to work at Firman's

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike is annoyed to find out Angie has employed Ida and Janice. Jim is sad that Steve does not want to work with him, and he plans to win Fiona back

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When the remains of a teenager are found half-buried in a wood, Vera finds herself investigating a 30-year-old mystery. Identified as Carrie Telling, a girl who disappeared during the 1984 miners' strike, the team retrieves news footage from the protests that shows her taking pictures in the background. It appears she got caught up in the unrest that day, contradicting her father's assertion that she went straight home. Photographs recovered from her camera yield further revelations for Vera. What did Carrie discover that ultimately led to her death?

programme.title: Quiz
programme.desc: Major Charles Ingram doesn't like quizzes, but for his wife Diana and brother-in-law Adrian knowing the answer is a way of life. As Diana and Adrian's repeated attempts to get into the hotseat on Who Wants to Be a Millionare? fall short, all eyes turn to an unsuspecting Charles to win the million-pound prize. Meanwhile, the repeated reappearance of middle-class, professional quizzers has not gone unnoticed by the ITV team and producer Paul Smith makes a vow to stop them by any means necessary. Fact-based drama directed by Stephen Frears, starring Matthew Macfadyen, Sian Clifford and Mark Bonnar, with Michael Sheen as Chris Tarrant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Despite arresting four people, the detectives face a wall of silence in their investigation into the grisly murders - but they eventually make a breakthrough, which doesn't quite give them the answers they were expecting. On the domestic front, Rachel wakes up in bed with charismatic colleague Sean - a mistake she is keen never to repeat - and Janet wants to keep her newly single status a secret

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A death in the family causes problems for Sid. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean become worried about their lovesick son, and invite the object of his affections to tea

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

But the festive mood changes when a man is stabbed to death with an antique sword during a ghost-hunting party at a spooky manor house in Morton Shallows. Local historian Brendan Pearce thinks blacksmith's daughter Rose Wilton is seeking revenge from beyond the grave, but Barnaby and sidekick Charlie Nelson try to find a more earthly motive behind the murder. Starring Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee, with Fiona Dolman, Les Dennis, Elizabeth Berrington, Mark Heap, Emily Joyce, James Murray and Hannah Tointon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the dramatic and beautiful landscape of the Lake District. Along the way he meets the man who made the world's longest Cumberland sausage, attempts to speed along the steepest road in Britain, crosses the Infinity Bridge and tries to break the record for the slowest water ski in history

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel and Jai speak to the kids. Tracy has a plan to reunite Leyla and Liam. Wendy is delighted by Victoria's offer, but Bob is disappointed

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel and Jai speak to the kids. Tracy has a plan to reunite Leyla and Liam. Wendy is delighted by Victoria's offer, but Bob is disappointed

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz entertains Maureen and Deirdre at Singles' Night at the Warwick. Gary rows with Judy for giving Zoe their money. Angie makes a pass at Chris

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen is alarmed when Fred keeps calling her. Deirdre is thrilled when Jon asks her out. Kevin is stunned when Natalie tells him she loves him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak takes advantage of Marlon being in charge at the wine bar. The Cairns family load up the car ready to leave for Germany. Alan tries to talk Eric out of his depression

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is furious about the mess the Dingles have caused at the tearooms. Steve is relieved when Kim agrees to sign his contract. Scott tells Lyn he's not happy in the army

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of bikers wreaks havoc in Aidensfield, prompting the locals to demand a new bobby. When young recruit PC Joe Mason (Joe McFadden) arrives he is not quite what they expected, with his tactics for crime fighting making his reception a mixed one between both residents and fellow police officers

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid takes a little exercise and Trevor decides to join him. Seventies sitcom, starring Sid James and Anthony Jackson

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park Cricket Club prepare for the opening ceremony of their new pitch and pavilion, Mim arranges a big surprise for Rog - but disaster is waiting in the wings. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: On the Buses (1971)
programme.desc: Wily bus driver Stan Butler thinks a new company policy that allows women behind the wheel will rob him of his precious overtime money. Enlisting the aid of conductor Jack, he does everything he can to sabotage the plan. Comedy, based on the TV series, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant, Stephen Lewis, Doris Hare, Peter Madden and Anna Karen

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A teenager arrives in the village asking for directions to Ashfordly Hall - and claims to be looking for her father. David decides to make a will, but becomes paranoid when one of the beneficiaries seems too eager to inherit, and a series of accidents befall him. Elsewhere, a new arrival at the Fossett farm is named after Joe. Guest starring Ophelia Lovibond

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan receives the injunction from Kim's solicitors. Zak embarks on another money-making scheme. Marlon is approached by the police for serving alcohol to under-age children

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe refuses to be bullied by Kim and Steve into giving Kelly a job. Emma insists that she does not want to go back to Germany. Tara confronts Steve about his dad

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin and Natalie plan to spend the night together. The McDonalds go to pick up Steve when he is released from prison. Alma tells Curly she still wants to work at Firman's

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike is annoyed to find out Angie has employed Ida and Janice. Jim is sad that Steve does not want to work with him, and he plans to win Fiona back

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When the remains of a teenager are found half-buried in a wood, Vera finds herself investigating a 30-year-old mystery. Identified as Carrie Telling, a girl who disappeared during the 1984 miners' strike, the team retrieves news footage from the protests that shows her taking pictures in the background. It appears she got caught up in the unrest that day, contradicting her father's assertion that she went straight home. Photographs recovered from her camera yield further revelations for Vera. What did Carrie discover that ultimately led to her death?

programme.title: Quiz
programme.desc: Major Charles Ingram doesn't like quizzes, but for his wife Diana and brother-in-law Adrian knowing the answer is a way of life. As Diana and Adrian's repeated attempts to get into the hotseat on Who Wants to Be a Millionare? fall short, all eyes turn to an unsuspecting Charles to win the million-pound prize. Meanwhile, the repeated reappearance of middle-class, professional quizzers has not gone unnoticed by the ITV team and producer Paul Smith makes a vow to stop them by any means necessary. Fact-based drama directed by Stephen Frears, starring Matthew Macfadyen, Sian Clifford and Mark Bonnar, with Michael Sheen as Chris Tarrant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Despite arresting four people, the detectives face a wall of silence in their investigation into the grisly murders - but they eventually make a breakthrough, which doesn't quite give them the answers they were expecting. On the domestic front, Rachel wakes up in bed with charismatic colleague Sean - a mistake she is keen never to repeat - and Janet wants to keep her newly single status a secret

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin introduces some of the great racehorses of the modern era, including Nijinsky, Tiger Roll, Best Mate, Desert Orchid and Shergar

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is assigned to protect an Middle Eastern diplomat who is being pursued by assassins. Bodie and Doyle find themselves relentlessly pursued by a gang of assailants that seems able to accurately anticipate their every move, leading to a tense car chase and a riverside shoot-out. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A former criminal and friend of Regan's wins a large amount of money on the pools - but then receives blackmailing phone calls regarding a crime from 11 years earlier for which he was never caught. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Edward Judd, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry gets a job chauffeuring the villainous Micky the Fish, but fears for his life when the gangster's mistress reports her jewels missing. Light-hearted crime caper, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents news ahead of a competitive day of racing at Ascot, plus a look ahead to key races over the Christmas period

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Action from February 1975, including Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City, Burnley v Sheffield United and Aston Villa v Manchester United

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: A look back at the 2020 campaign, which started at Donington Park and concluded in Brands Hatch, with 27 races taking place over a period of less than four months

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 2.25 Grade One Marsh Hurdle from Ascot, which is followed by races at 3.00 and 3.35. Plus, two races from Haydock Park, including the 2.40 Tommy Whittle Chase. With analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and AP McCoy, reports by Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey, Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Return of the Seven (1966)
programme.desc: The leader of a gang of bandits enslaves all the men from a Mexican village while maddened with grief over the death of his son. However, one of his hostages is a survivor from the original team of gunfighters, and his wife persuades his two remaining companions to form a new band of heroes and mount a rescue mission. Western sequel, starring Yul Brynner, Robert Fuller and Warren Oates

programme.desc: A look back at a selection of sports covered by ITV during the 1980s, ranging from football and boxing to wrestling and double decker racing

programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the second semi-final on day six from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Neil Robertson and Graeme Dott both prevailed 6-4 against Kyren Wilson and Tom Ford respectively at this stage when the tournament was last held in February at Cheltenham, with Robertson going on to win the title. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Neal Foulds and Ken Doherty, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Safe House (2012)
programme.desc: A CIA agent stationed in South Africa is sent to question a rogue operative, who has been arrested years after betraying the agency, only to be attacked by mercenaries hired to capture the fugitive. As they flee for their lives, the interrogator has to contend with not only the enemies hunting them, but also the mind games of his prisoner. Thriller, with Denzel Washington and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: Featuring the new Vauxhall Mokka and a new saloon car from Kia

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry protects a widowed garage owner from her grasping family and Arthur spots a chance to achieve one of his greatest ambitions - getting his hands on a Rolls-Royce. Lionel Jeffries and Keith Barron guest star alongside Dennis Waterman and George Cole

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Newly promoted Captain Horatio Hornblower sets out to make his mark on the world, and soon manages to capture his first enemy vessel - only to discover a peace treaty has been signed, leaving him down and out, with barely a penny to his name. Fate seems to smile on the ambitious seafarer though, when he discovers the villainous Bonaparte has reneged on the treaty. Drama based on the CS Forester novels, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay, Paul McGann, Greg Wise, Lorcan Cranitch and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: The Horse Soldiers (1959)
programme.desc: A Union cavalry officer is ordered to demolish a railway junction in the middle of Confederate territory, but finds his authority constantly challenged by a military surgeon assigned to accompany him. John Ford's Western, based on an actual Civil War incident, starring John Wayne, William Holden, Constance Towers, Hoot Gibson and Anna Lee

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of sports covered by ITV, including horse racing, Formula One and darts

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Colombia to search for a fish suspected of dragging a honeymooning bride to her death in the Amazon. A key witness turns the investigation on its head after revealing the body was found with a single puncture wound, and Jeremy conducts an experiment to prove his unlikely new suspect is capable of pulling an adult under the water

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey present the competitive angling show, joined by ex-Liverpool and Spurs footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock and former middleweight boxing champion Steve 'the Celtic Warrior' Collins. They all head back to Ali's fishing roots in Essex as the journey turns into one of humour, drama, and plenty of coarse fish, as well as a crazy sea fishing challenge to finish

programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the second session of the final on day seven from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Neil Robertson of Australia overcame Scotland's Graeme Dott 10-8 to win the title when the tournament was last held at Cheltenham in February, becoming the fifth different winner in the six stagings of the tournament. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Neal Foulds and Ken Doherty, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: National Lampoon's European Vacation (1985)
programme.desc: The disaster-prone Griswolds appear on a TV game show, and through a happy accident, end up winning a holiday touring Europe. The trip takes them to Britain, France and Germany, causing mayhem wherever they go, while the final destination in Rome leads to a run-in with a gang of thieves. Comedy sequel, starring Chevy Chase and Beverly D'Angelo

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: Members of the Kerry Mountain Rescue team head to Mangerton mountain after receiving a call about an elderly walker in Killarney National Park who has complained of chest pains. On the Humber estuary, a pleasure boat suffers engine failure and Humber rescue team members speed to its aid while it drifts perilously close to some rocks

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: Documentary focused on rescue efforts across the British Isles, featuring access to Mountain Rescue teams, Coast Guard and air ambulances. In the first edition, a rescue team in Kerry is called to Carrantuohil, the highest mountain in Ireland, to rescue an American couple who have got lost and are experiencing some of the worst weather Ireland can produce. On the Humber estuary, the Humber Inshore Lifeboat is called to a reported body on the banks of the river, but the job turns out to be a false alarm

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: An inebriated man collapses in reception, and Figgis is employed to find out more about the mysterious visitor

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: The boys are dismissed from hospital and promptly arrange a reunion under more enjoyable circumstances

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar plays his part in a gangster's plot to trick his four greatest enemies into carrying out a robbery. Stephanie Beacham features in an early role, alongside Roger Moore

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo investigate the killings of a drug dealer and an SI-10 officer and are soon on the trail of an assassin hired by a powerful American crimelord. Drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry gets a job chauffeuring the villainous Micky the Fish, but fears for his life when the gangster's mistress reports her jewels missing. Light-hearted crime caper, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to keep a surveillance on Cowley after evidence suggests he has stolen two canisters of a lethal nerve gas and begun negotiating with an unfriendly foreign power. The plot thickens when they trace their boss to a country mansion, where after they are caught he denies knowing them

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The son of an ex-informant is kidnapped and the Flying Squad is brought in to assist in the hunt. Against Regan's advice his father, a wealthy businessman, pays the ransom and the boy is returned. However, the reunion quickly turns sour. Joss Ackland guest stars, with John Thaw

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates when a trusted diplomat friend goes missing with $1million, while his daughter is adducted. Robert Hardy guest stars, with Roger Moore

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A woman asks Holmes to search for her missing husband, a businessman who was last seen peering from the window of an opium den in London's notorious Limehouse area. Suspicion falls on a well-known local beggar - but his innocence is eventually proved in bizarre fashion. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo probe a colleague's death, which brings to light the officer's investigations into the activities of right-wing extremists

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's latest money-making idea involves a racing scam with an imaginary office. He hires a postal address and uses three public telephone boxes, and Terry has to make sure they are kept free. Drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is faced with a terrifying campaign of explosions and assassinations, apparently committed by an opposing operative that Cowley is convinced he killed several years earlier. It appears that he is either mistaken or an old associate of his former adversary is seeking revenge. Gordon Jackson stars

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Ullswater in the Lake District, the Patterdale Mountain Rescue team is called to assist a man who has broken his leg on the far shore of the lake, the second longest in the UK. Thankfully, the team has a high-speed boat in which to rush to his aid. In the Gap of Dunloe County in Kerry, a 22-year-old climber has fallen 75ft and hit a boulder. He has regained consciousness but has broken several bones and his pelvis. The Kerry Mountain Rescue Team, National Ambulance service and Irish Coast Guard helicopter winch him 200ft on a stretcher to the hovering helicopter

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Croagh Patrick in Co Mayo, the Mayo Mountain Rescue Team gears up for the annual religious pilgrimage, where up to 30,000 people set out to reach the summit of the mountain. More than 120 volunteers from other rescue teams, the Order of Malta, Civil Defence and Irish Air Corps assist the many injured pilgrims as they fall, have heart attacks and break ankles. On the Kinder Plateau, the Buxton and Kinder Mountain Rescue teams are called to an injured walker, while in the Lake District, three mountain rescue teams attend an injured ice climber with spinal and head injuries

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at a Welsh factory creating tinsel, a team hand-rolling festive crackers, how Christmas puddings are made to last and how advent calendars are created

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Riding an historic Tiger Moth bi-plane, Griff Rhys Jones arrives in the humid tropics of Darwin. He encounters a crocodile and an iconic news editor, before travelling through the night on a road train, arriving in the 'red centre' of Alice Springs - only to be surprised by the lush green sporting fields and the mysterious Americans playing baseball there. The presenter gets some tips on how to survive in the desert from an Aboriginal elder, before riding into the sunset on a camel - in tribute to the Afghan cameleers for which the legendary Ghan passenger train is named

programme.title: The World's End (2013)
programme.desc: A 40-year-old man who refuses to grow up drags four reluctant childhood friends back to their home town to recreate a pub crawl they attempted in their teens. As they work their way round the 12 local bars, they stumble on a threat to the entire human race. Sci-fi comedy, starring Simon Pegg, Nick Frost and Martin Freeman

programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the second semi-final on day six from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. Fate seems to smile on the ambitious seafarer though, when he discovers the villainous Bonaparte has reneged on the treaty. programme.title: Live Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the second session of the final on day seven from the Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes - the first of three tournaments that make up the ITV Series. programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is faced with a terrifying campaign of explosions and assassinations, apparently committed by an opposing operative that Cowley is convinced he killed several years earlier. programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Ullswater in the Lake District, the Patterdale Mountain Rescue team is called to assist a man who has broken his leg on the far shore of the lake, the second longest in the UK. Thankfully, the team has a high-speed boat in which to rush to his aid. In the Gap of Dunloe County in Kerry, a 22-year-old climber has fallen 75ft and hit a boulder. He has regained consciousness but has broken several bones and his pelvis. The Kerry Mountain Rescue Team, National Ambulance service and Irish Coast Guard helicopter winch him 200ft on a stretcher to the hovering helicopter

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Croagh Patrick in Co Mayo, the Mayo Mountain Rescue Team gears up for the annual religious pilgrimage, where up to 30,000 people set out to reach the summit of the mountain. More than 120 volunteers from other rescue teams, the Order of Malta, Civil Defence and Irish Air Corps assist the many injured pilgrims as they fall, have heart attacks and break ankles. On the Kinder Plateau, the Buxton and Kinder Mountain Rescue teams are called to an injured walker, while in the Lake District, three mountain rescue teams attend an injured ice climber with spinal and head injuries

programme.title: Made in Britain

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Riding an historic Tiger Moth bi-plane, Griff Rhys Jones arrives in the humid tropics of Darwin. He encounters a crocodile and an iconic news editor, before travelling through the night on a road train, arriving in the 'red centre' of Alice Springs - only to be surprised by the lush green sporting fields and the mysterious Americans playing baseball there. The presenter gets some tips on how to survive in the desert from an Aboriginal elder, before riding into the sunset on a camel - in tribute to the Afghan cameleers for which the legendary Ghan passenger train is named

programme.title: The World's End (2013)

programme.title: Botched
programme.desc: Dr Dubrow and Dr Nassif present a holiday countdown of their most over-the-top patients, including a human cartoon, viral butt implants, and a dynamic duo looking for their next surgeries.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Atlanta

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer reluctantly pawns the TV to pay for family counselling sessions - but Dr Marvin Monroe's shocking treatment yields unexpected results

programme.title: Escape from Planet Earth (2013)
programme.desc: A heroic alien space explorer travels to Earth in response to a distress call. When he ends up being captured by the military and imprisoned in the infamous Area 51, it falls to his timid, nerdy brother to save him. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Rob Corddry, Brendan Fraser and Sarah Jessica Parker

programme.title: Heineken Champions Cup Rugby: The Big Tackle
programme.desc: Ed Jackson and guests present highlights and news from around the European rugby world, including a look ahead to this afternoon's game between Ulster and Gloucester

programme.title: The Bear
programme.desc: A large furry bear arrives from the zoo to take up temporary residence with a little girl, Tilly, and her parents. Children's animation based on the Raymond Briggs tale, with the vocal talents of Charlotte Church

programme.title: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
programme.desc: Animated adventure, based on the popular children's bedtime book by Michael Rosen and Helen Oxenbury. Stan, Katie, Rosie, Max and the baby, along with Rufus the dog, all embark on a quest to find some bears. Trekking through whirling snowstorms, muddy bogs and dark forests, the siblings refuse to let anything get in their way. However, when Rosie and Rufus end up getting lost, bear-hunting suddenly doesn't seem quite so much fun after all. Featuring the voices of Olivia Colman, Pam Ferris and Mark Williams as Mum, Grandma and Dad respectively

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Harry Hill hosts as the next 10 contestants enter the tent to face a technical challenge involving multi-coloured batter, and then have to bake cakes to represent their families

programme.title: World Chase Tag - USA
programme.desc: Highlights from the final in Atlanta, Georgia, as the teams compete for the title in a knockout version of the playground game

programme.title: Live Heineken Champions Cup Rugby Union
programme.desc: Leinster v Northampton Saints (Kick-off 1.00pm). Lee McKenzie presents coverage of the match in the second round of Pool A fixtures, which takes place at RDS Arena in Dublin. Leinster are on the hunt for a record fifth title and the Saints are looking to avenge a defeat in the 2011 final, when Leinster came back from 22-6 down at half time to snatch the cup out of their hands with a 33-22 triumph. With analysis from Nolli Waterman, reports by Ed Jackson and commentary from Miles Harrison

programme.title: Jamie's Easy Christmas Countdown
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers his stress-free guide to planning the most important feast of the year, by showing exactly what to cook and when across Christmas Eve and Christmas Day. At the centre of his table is a show-stopping turkey with herb butter and crispy skin. That's surrounded by smoked salmon pate, pork stuffing crammed with silky leeks, sweet peaches and roasted chestnuts, glazed clementine carrots and gnarly sausage and sage

programme.title: Christmas at Chatsworth House
programme.desc: One-off documentary going behind the scenes of Chatsworth, one of England's most impressive stately homes, as the Duke, Duchess and their 800 staff brace themselves for an influx of 200,000 visitors over the 2019 festive season. Christmas is the most important event for the Derbyshire estate and crucial to its survival. In just five days, the house is transformed with 30 Christmas trees, 60,000 baubles, and a million fairy lights. But this year Derbyshire has been crippled by unprecedented flooding. Can family and staff rally to make this a season to be remembered?

programme.title: Jungle Mystery: Lost Kingdoms of the Amazon
programme.desc: Ella Al-Shamahi joins a complex police operation deep in the Amazon, as the authorities attempt to clamp down on illegal mining, which is poisoning the ecosystem of the rainforest. When the Spanish arrived in the Amazon 500 years ago, indigenous populations were devastated by disease and enslavement. Today's descendants of the Amazon's once flourishing civilisation are facing the same threats as a hostile world closes around them. Ella joins indigenous groups who are trying to fight back, formally marking land borders and using mobile phone technology to listen for illegal logging in the forest

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Barenboim on Beethoven
programme.desc: A remarkable insight into the mind and music of Ludwig van Beethoven, featuring highlights from Daniel Barenboim's 1970 series of programmes about the man and his music. Shot at London's Roundhouse Theatre, this compilation cherry picks some of the best moments, as Barenboim talks and plays extracts at the piano, illustrating the defining characteristics of Beethoven's compositions and sharing his personal insight into the composer's mindset. The full series of 13 episodes in their entirety - over seven hours, from the Fourth Piano Concerto to the Eroica to Beethoven's Fifth - follows on More4

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Manchester to explore the spirit of protest and revolution that grew out of the experience of Britain's working class during the Industrial Revolution. At the John Ryland Library, Alice is granted access to rarely-seen period documents that highlight the true human cost of the Peterloo massacre. At the Free Trade Hall, she explores how Manchester found itself at the heart of the battle against the hated Corn Laws, and at Chetham's Library, Roberts sits at the very desk where Friedrich Engels and Karl Marx, inspired by the living conditions of Manchester's working poor, began the discussions and research that would lead to the publication of the Communist Manifesto

programme.title: Forrest Gump (1994)
programme.desc: A slow-witted man has a series of bizarre adventures, including becoming an American football star, Vietnam veteran, table tennis champion and millionaire businessman - but the love of his life from childhood continues to elude him. Robert Zemeckis's Oscar-winning comedy drama, starring Tom Hanks, Sally Field, Robin Wright and Gary Sinise

programme.title: Carrie (1976)
programme.desc: A shy, withdrawn teenager discovers she has paranormal powers, which she uses to protect herself from her abusive mother and classmates. When a friendly student invites her to the prom, she is determined to enjoy herself and is amazed when she is crowned queen of the ball - but joy turns to fury when her tormentors play a cruel prank. Brian De Palma's adaptation of Stephen King's horror novel, with Sissy Spacek, Piper Laurie and Amy Irving

programme.desc: A pair of former soldiers start a new career as nightclub entertainers. They try to boost the popularity of their old commanding officer's struggling winter resort in Vermont by staging a series of extravagant shows, with a little help from their former army buddies and a talented singing sister act. Musical, starring Bing Crosby, Danny Kaye, Vera-Ellen, Dean Jagger, Sig Rumann, Mary Wickes, Anne Whitfield and Rosemary Clooney, featuring seasonal songs by Irving Berlin

programme.title: The Man Who Invented Christmas (2017)
programme.desc: It's 1843, and struggling with debt after penning three novels which found little favour with the public, Charles Dickens needs a commercial hit to support his family. So he begins work on a new book entitled A Christmas Carol. Comedy drama, starring Dan Stevens, Christopher Plummer and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Luxury Cruises: From Boom to Bust
programme.desc: Kate Quilton examines how ocean travel was transformed from a simple journey to a luxurious holiday in itself. Once the preserve of the wealthy, in the 1980s and 1990s the cruise industry became one of the most extraordinary boom stories, making luxury affordable to the masses. Then Covid shut the industry down. Joined by experts, Kate explores the boom years, the challenges and disasters that rocked the industry, and also considers the post-pandemic future

programme.title: Royal Antiques Revived
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell meets three royal repairers as they embark on a journey of incredible restoration. From King George IV's piano to a car that's believed to have belonged to the Queen Mother, and a very rare copy of King Louis XV roll-top desk, every item provides a glimpse into the lives of the people who owned them and the restorers working on them

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall

programme.title: Home Alone: Britain's Favourite Christmas Movie
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the 1990 festive classic written by John Hughes. It's a movie that turned its then-nine-year-old star, Macaulay Culkin, into the biggest child actor since Shirley Temple. Featuring interviews with film critics, fans, stuntmen and even a reformed burglar, who assesses the home security at the Home Alone house

programme.title: When Christmas Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Noddy Holder narrates this extended special looking at the unplanned, unwanted but often hilarious moments that have beset the world of TV during the festive season. Festive cock-ups include Christmas trees that won't stand up and kids' toys that won't shut up, via the worst yuletide movie ever and some distinctly misguided TV adverts. Clips feature a host of stars, including Phillip Schofield, Eamonn Holmes and iconic TV chef Fanny Cradock, while things go horribly wrong for Piers Morgan at a Christmas lights switch-on and singer Patti LaBelle forgets the words in front of then-US president Bill Clinton

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the famous gold-and-green facade of the London retailer, revealing the store's origins and the secrets of its success. Viewed as a uniquely British institution for more than 150 years, items on sale range from precious gems and high-class toys to designer fashions - meaning it remains a popular choice for shoppers wanting to indulge at Christmas. Former grotto staff share their secrets about what the super-rich might ask of Santa, while former employees reveal what it is like looking after celebrities and royalty during the festive season

programme.title: Inside Lidl at Christmas
programme.desc: With Britain facing its toughest December in memory, this programme goes behind the doors of the budget supermarket to find out how it is preparing for Christmas - and how it keeps its prices down. The Wynne family put Lidl through its paces by trying out its turkey and trimmings, there's a Christmas pudding taste test and a look at the store's decorations and random gifts

programme.title: 5 News Update

programme.desc: Festive celebrations with Jane McDonald, and Amanda and Clive Owen and family from Our Yorkshire Farm. Plus, there are contributions from Julian Norton and Peter Wright from The Yorkshire Vet, and Friday on the Farm stars Rob and Dave Nicholson. As the people of Harrogate get their town ready for the Christmas festivities, cameras follow busy workers from the county doing their bit

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: Hill shepherds Amanda and Clive Owen live on one of the most remote farms in Britain with their nine children. Cameras follow them as they cope with one of the worst winters in 50 years, and fight to protect their 1,000-strong flock of sheep

programme.title: Victoria Wood: In Her Own Words
programme.desc: A look back at one of Britain's best loved comedians, featuring interviews with Jo Brand, Jenny Eclair, Gyles Brandreth, Susie Blake and the actors who worked alongside Victoria Wood. The programme charts her rise from her upbringing in Lancashire, via her partnership with Julie Walters on Wood and Walters, As Seen On TV, and her one-woman shows that sold out London's Royal Albert Hall

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Christmas Ads
programme.desc: From cat food to Coca-Cola, stocking fillers to stock cubes, Christmas has provided the perfect opportunity to sell the viewing public everything and anything. Here, Julian Clary remembers the good, the great, and the unforgettable. Bill Oddie looks back at his 1980s Christmas Woolworth ads, and Jason Bradbury remembers how the commercials for Action Man made it the must-have toy of the 1970s. Others looking back include Oz Clarke, Anita Harris, Rustie Lee, Hal Cruttenden, Lucy Porter, Natalie Haynes and Mark Borkowski

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain. He begins in the cab of Puffing Billy, the world's oldest-surviving steam locomotive, which was built in 1813 and designed to move coal along a five-mile stretch of track from a Northumberland mine to the docks. Chris also tells the story of Henry Booth, who not only championed George Stephenson's famous Rocket, but also helped finance it, and talks about the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity

Oscar-winning Western, directed by and starring Kevin Costner. With Mary McDonnell, Graham Greene, Rodney A Grant and Floyd Red Crow Westerman

programme.desc: Sue Johnston narrates a look at the life and career of the actor, the star of Only Fools and Horses, The Darling Buds of May, A Touch of Frost and Open All Hours. The programme examines his early stage career and hear what his fellow thespians thought of him, and there are clips of him in long-forgotten shows, quirky TV adverts and his very first movie. Featuring interviews with industry insiders and former castmates, including Tessa Peake-Jones, who played his long-suffering on-screen wife Raquel in Only Fools and Horses

Including Steve Martin causing chaos with a pair of scissors on Parkinson, a puppy taking a shine to Gordon the Gopher on Going Live! and Noel Edmonds suffering an injury live in front of millions on The Late Late Breakfast Show
